Â Â Mariateresa Vivaldini (ECR). â Signor Presidente, Commissario, onorevoli colleghi, condividiamo l'ambizione che emerge dall'Affordable Energy Action Plan di perseguire la sovranitÃ  energetica, considerando le specificitÃ  dei singoli Stati membri.

Ci preoccupano perÃ² le strategie per realizzarla. Viviamo in un'epoca di contraddizioni, Commissario, e il fatto che la Francia abbia aumentato dell'81% i flussi di gas naturale liquefatto russo nel 2024 Ã¨ una di queste, soprattutto se paragonata agli sforzi di Paesi come l'Italia per aprire nuovi canali di approvvigionamento.

Ecco, non vorrei che queste contraddizioni contaminino l'obiettivo finale: staccarci per sempre dal gas russo. Ad oggi la Commissione ha annunciato e rinviato a data da destinarsi il piano per il phase out. Non indago sulle ragioni, ma non le sembra una contraddizione?

Quanto alle soluzioni, le rinnovabili non bastano. Serve una strategia per il nucleare e investimenti in ricerca sulla fusione; serve l'idrogeno, servono i biocarburanti, serve il coraggio di estrarre le riserve di gas che abbiamo in Europa ma che non sfruttiamo.

Coraggio, ecco, Ã¨ la parola chiave: Ã¨ la chiave per vincere quella che, a mio avviso, Ã¨ una sfida epocale su cui si gioca l'esistenza stessa dell'Europa.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Katri Kulmuni (Renew). â MrÂ President, if we wish honestly to be selfâsufficient and independent in security, then defence isn't enough. The only way to be really selfâsufficient, independent and strategically autonomous is to also be independent when it comes to local food and energy production.

While the EU doesn't pose significant fossil fuels, the only way to truly secure our backs is by strong independent defence, an independent and renewable energy sector and local food production.

And I hope that thereÂ are no forces here that wish to return to fossil energy, for as long as you rely on fossilâbased energy solutions, one cannot be truly selfâsufficient. Hopefully, not a single country wishes to be relying on Russian gas, but not only on Russian gas â the lifeline network with any other fossil energy producer is not promoting our European selfâsufficiency neither.

So if you're going to be cold or dependent on imported food or armoury, as long you can be oppressed. And that is not the way forward for Europeans; the way forward is with strong defence, renewable energy and local food production.

Med den andra handen ger vi pengar direkt till fienden och Ã¶kar hotet mot oss sjÃ¤lva. Det mÃ¥ste vara nog med det nu.

Tre Ã¥r in i kriget kan vi fortfarande inte garantera att EU-pengar inte gÃ¥r till att bomba skolor i Ukraina. Importen av den ryska energin mÃ¥ste upphÃ¶ra omedelbart. Allt annat Ã¤r ett sjÃ¤lvskadebeteende.

Â Â

Â Â Mika Aaltola (PPE). â MrÂ President,Â two years ago, Russia's brutal invasion of Ukraine demanded a unified European response. We declared a bold ambition to sever our dependency on Russian natural gas by 2027. Yet a stark reality reveals a troubling fact: we are falling short.

Russian LNG imports into EU have increased recently, according to some reports. This is not merely a statistical anomaly; it is a strategic vulnerability. Exemptions for pipeline crude oil and the insidious refining loophole continue to funnel vital revenue into Russia.

Let us be clear: we cannot credibly rearm Europe while our energy dependence fuels the very aggression we seek to deter.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â NicolÃ¡s GonzÃ¡lez Casares (S&D). â SeÃ±or presidente, seÃ±or comisario, debemos actuar con inteligencia. Cada electrÃ³n producido en Europa, cada molÃ©cula verde producida en Europa, es menos beneficio para Rusia y para Putin. Por lo tanto, debemos reforzar el plan REPowerEU y apoyar una hoja de ruta clara para reducir la dependencia energÃ©tica de Rusia.

Al mismo tiempo, comisario, le agradezco que haya establecido como una de sus principales prioridades la eliminaciÃ³n de las dependencias energÃ©ticas de Rusia, las de petrÃ³leo, las de gas, por barco, tambiÃ©n por gaseoducto y las de uranio âtambiÃ©n hay que recordarloâ.

TambiÃ©n entiendo que los recientes terremotos geopolÃ­ticos hayan retrasado algo el plan; pero debemos ser inteligentes en esta reducciÃ³n de dependencia. Hay que cortar el gas con Rusia, pero saber que nos vamos a tener que defender de aquellos que quieren manejar ese suministro.Â No estÃ¡ bien salir de una dependencia y caer en la dependencia de otros que tambiÃ©n quieren jugar con los precios del gas.

AdemÃ¡s, debemos poner en marcha medidas de emergencia, vÃ¡lvulas de escape, porque cuando suben los precios del gas tambiÃ©n suben automÃ¡ticamente los de la electricidad. Tendremos que saber defendernos en esas situaciones, pero cortemos ya la dependencia con Rusia y hagÃ¡moslo de manera inteligente.

Y, por Ãºltimo, no a Nord Stream 2, que quede claro ya.

Â

Â Â Csaba DÃ¶mÃ¶tÃ¶r (PfE), kÃ©kkÃ¡rtyÃ¡s vÃ¡lasz. â Nagyon kÃ¶szÃ¶nÃ¶m a kÃ©rdÃ©sÃ©t. Â Â Piotr MÃ¼ller (ECR). â Panie PrzewodniczÄcy! Szanowni PaÅstwo! Niech zlikwidujÄ, niech zniszczÄ gazociÄgi Nord Stream i Nord Stream 2 skutecznie. Niech wycofajÄ siÄ z jego dalszej eksploatacji w przyszÅoÅci. Â Â Stine Bosse (Renew). â Hr. Formand! NÃ¥r man lytter til nogen her i Europa-Parlamentet, kan man bedre forstÃ¥, at vi stadig bruger russisk gas. Jeg behÃ¸ver ikke nÃ¦vne navne. Vi ved jo godt, hvem det er. Der sidder nogen her, som ikke mener, at Putin har gjort noget som helst galt, og som i Ã¸vrigt mener, at dÃ¸ren skal stÃ¥ pÃ¥ vid gab. Det efterlader et stÃ¸rre arbejde og ansvar til os andre, og det skal vi tage pÃ¥ os. Hver dag vi importerer russisk gas, betaler vi for de missiler, som rammer familiers hjem. Det skal stoppe, koste hvad det vil. Alle lande skal stoppe import af russisk gas, og vi skal stÃ¸tte, at det bliver muligt nu. Vi skal sÃ¦tte fart pÃ¥ udbygningen af anden energi, vind i nord og sol i syd, brint og Power-to-X og, hvad det hedder alt sammen. Og vi skal investere massivt i Europas energiinfrastruktur, sÃ¥ vi kan transportere energien i Europa. SÃ¥dan bliver vi frie af russisk gas. SÃ¥dan hjÃ¦lper vi ukrainerne. SÃ¥dan hjÃ¦lper vi klimaet, og sÃ¥dan bevarer vi vores egen frihed.

Â Â

Â Â Jutta Paulus (Verts/ALE). â Mr President, honourable Commissioner, dear colleagues, it has been three years now since REPowerEU was launched and the promise to end fossil fuel imports is still not fulfilled. Twice has the Commission announced a roadmap, and yet we still wait for a concrete proposal.

Yes, a lot has been achieved. Gas imports have decreased by two thirds, being replaced mostly by renewables and efficiency, which is a much more viable solution than replacing an old dependency with a new one, especially considering the slippery slope US democracy has gotten on.

Oil and coal are sanctioned, thus reaching the EU now via the ominous shadow fleet or via third countries. So what I want to see in the Commission's roadmap â besides, of course, doubling down on renewables and efficiency â is a clear concept to trace imports back to their source. We have to end the whitewashing of bloodstained Russian energy commodities.

Â 
Â Â

Â Â Emma Wiesner (Renew), blue-card question. â My question is simple: do you want to end the war? Because if you want to end the war, it's quite clear that the more finance, the more euros we put in Putin's pockets, the harder it would be for Ukraine to win.

So, sanctioning Russian energy, sanctioning the oil and gas â well, it's the obvious answer. If we want to end the war, we cannot finance a war. So, do you really want to end it?

Â 
Â Â

Â Â Maximilian Krah (NI). â Herr PrÃ¤sident, meine Damen und Herren! Ich muss zugeben, nach diesen Debatten bin ich erstaunt, wie disconnected wir von der RealitÃ¤t sind. Es laufen die FriedensgesprÃ¤che in Saudi-Arabien. Sind Sie darauf vorbereitet, dass der Krieg ein Ende haben kann? Und was machen Sie dann mit Ihrer Kriegsrhetorik?

Und wenn wir Ã¼ber Energie sprechenÂ â Energie hat auch einen Preis. Es ist nun einmal ein Fakt, dass es keine preiswertere Energie gibt als Erdgas aus Pipelines. Und wenn Sie Energie teurer kaufen wollen, dann sind wir nicht mehr konkurrenzfÃ¤hig. Und wenn Sie zugleich aufrÃ¼sten wollen, dann mÃ¼ssen Sie produzieren. Wie wollen Sie produzieren, wenn Ihre Energiekosten nicht konkurrenzfÃ¤hig sind? Alles, was Sie hier bieten, ist eine RealitÃ¤t, die nicht existiertÂ â das ist eine Scheinwirklichkeit.

Und genau deshalb ist diese EU irrelevant. Deshalb verhandelt man ohne die EU. Deshalb bekommt Kaja Kallas keinen Termin im State Department, weil man sagt: Mit Leuten, die im Zauberwald leben, mit denen reden wir nicht, wir reden nur mit denen, die in der RealitÃ¤t sind.

Deshalb nutze ich die Chance, mich heute hier von Ihnen zu verabschieden. Ich gehe dahin, wo die RealitÃ¤t jetzt wieder gemacht wirdÂ â in den nationalen Parlamenten, im Deutschen Bundestag.

(Der Redner ist damit einverstanden, auf eine Frage nach dem Verfahren der âblauen Karteâ zu antworten.)

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Jutta Paulus (Verts/ALE), Antwort auf eine Frage nach dem Verfahren der âblauen Karteâ. â Ich wollte nur wissen, ob Sie inzwischen auf derÂ payroll von Putin stehen, weil bisher war es ja China, die Sie unterstÃ¼tzt haben.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Maximilian Krah (NI), Antwort auf eine Frage nach dem Verfahren der âblauen Karteâ. â Wissen Sie, diese Frage offenbart die gesamte Argumentationslosigkeit, die Sie haben. Weil Sie keine Sachargumente haben, werden Sie persÃ¶nlich und billig. Die Wahrheit ist sehr einfach: Ich unterstÃ¼tze Donald Trump, der die Wirklichkeit endlich verÃ¤ndert, der einen Plan hat, den Krieg zu beenden. Wir wollen Frieden, und wir wollen nicht Ihre billige Rhetorik ohne strategisches Ziel.

Sie haben keinen Plan fÃ¼r die Industrie, Sie haben keinen Plan fÃ¼r die Energie. Was Sie wollen, ist Krieg foreverÂ â da sind wir nicht dabei. Und wenn Sie nichts anderes haben als einen peinlichen Ad-hominem-Angriff, dann beweisen Sie, dass wir richtig sind und Sie Ã¼berwunden.

Deshalb gewinnen wir Wahlen, und Sie losen ab.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â JÃ¼ri Ratas (PPE). â MrÂ President, dear colleagues, Commissioner, Russia's brutal and illegal war against Ukraine has continued for the fourth year, but Europe still depends on Russian gas. It is unacceptable that imports of LNG from Russia to the European Union have even increased, reaching record levels earlier this year.

By continuing to buy Russian cars, Europe is giving the Kremlin billions of dollars that it can use to finance its evil deeds against humanity. It is well known that Moscow is using the money to bomb Ukraine, its cities and villages, its homes and people, hospitals and infrastructure. Those EU countries that continue to import Russian cars are also responsible for this.

This must stop. We must do whatever it takes to weaken the Russian war machine.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Bruno Tobback (S&D). â Mr President, Commissioner, colleagues, Europe has performed a remarkable feat in reducing its dependency on Russian gas and oil after the attack on Ukraine. But the work is only half done. Not only are we still importing Russian gas, but by simply switching to other suppliers, we have found little more than a quick fix. Because if depending on Vladimir Putin was naive, then repeating the mistake by making our economies dependent on a gas cartel between Vladimir Putin and Donald Trump would be outright irresponsible.

Some in this Parliament do not like the idea of reducing fossil fuel use; it reminds them too much of Greta Thunberg, and she scares them. And since we are all woke, we must respect their feelings. But we cannot change geology, and that means that any European economy dependent on gas or oil will eventually find itself in the shoes of President Zelenskyy â facing blackmail and extortion, whether it be in the Oval Office or at the end of a very long table in the Kremlin.

So, colleagues, let us at least spare our economies, our businesses and our citizens that fate by making the transition work.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Lena Schilling (Verts/ALE). â Herr PrÃ¤sident, liebe Kolleginnen und Kollegen! Zuerst mÃ¶chte ich uns alle beglÃ¼ckwÃ¼nschen, dass (Ton aus) dieses Parlament verlÃ¤sst.

Und nun zur Sache. Ja, wir diskutieren zu Recht Ã¼ber unsere Sicherheit. Wir diskutieren zu Recht darÃ¼ber, wie wir die Ukraine unterstÃ¼tzen, und zu Recht Ã¼ber Sanktionen gegen RusslandÂ â aber dann mÃ¼ssen wir schon auch konsequent sein. Und konsequent sein heiÃt, uns nicht mehr wirtschaftlich abhÃ¤ngig zu machen von Russland, von Putin und auch nicht von Gas- und Ãlexporten von anderen Autokraten; dann mÃ¼ssen wir konsequent sein und drei Jahre nach Putins brutalem Angriffskrieg feststellen, dass wir vielleicht ein bisschen spÃ¤t dran sind.

Vorgestern wÃ¤re der beste Moment gewesen, um aus Fossilen auszusteigen und diese AbhÃ¤ngigkeit zu beenden. Aber heute? Heute ist vielleicht der zweitbeste Moment, das zu tun und nicht mehr einzuknicken. Weil es geopolitisch richtig ist, weil es klimapolitisch richtig ist und weil es die einzige Entscheidung ist, die wir treffen kÃ¶nnen, wenn wir an eine europÃ¤ische, gemeinsame, starke, solidarische Zukunft glauben.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Andrea Wechsler (PPE). â Herr PrÃ¤sident, sehr geehrter Herr Kommissar JÃ¸rgensen! Russlands Krieg gegen die Ukraine hat uns sehr schmerzhaft vor Augen gefÃ¼hrt, dass Europas EnergieabhÃ¤ngigkeit von autokratischen Regimen ein strategischer Fehler war. Und die EuropÃ¤ische Union hat darauf reagiertÂ â und das erfolgreichÂ â, und sie wird es weiterhin tun; herzlichen Dank auch dafÃ¼r.

Aber lassen Sie mich eines klarstellen: Die entscheidende Frage ist nicht nur der phase-out von russischen fossilen EnergietrÃ¤gern. Die entscheidende Frage ist: Wie stellen wir sicher, dass wir nicht von der einen AbhÃ¤ngigkeit in die nÃ¤chste geraten? Wir dÃ¼rfen nicht den Fehler begehen, in neue AbhÃ¤ngigkeiten zu geraten. Und wenn wir Ã¼ber erneuerbare Energien reden, dÃ¼rfen wir nicht vergessen, dass es jÃ¼ngst auch Warnungen Ã¼ber die Risiken in chinesischen Wechselrichtern in europÃ¤ischen PV- und Windkraftanlagen und damit in unserer kritischen Infrastruktur gegeben hatÂ â und das muss uns ein Weckruf sein. Wer unsere Energieversorgung auch digital kontrollieren kann, der hat die Kontrolle Ã¼ber unsere Energiesicherheit.

Und deswegen ist die Zeit geopolitischer NaivitÃ¤t vorbei. Wir mÃ¼ssen auch an uns denken: die eigenen KapazitÃ¤ten aufbauen und unsere eigene Energietechnologie und âproduktion voranbringen.

Â Â Thomas Pellerin-Carlin (S&D). â Monsieur le PrÃ©sident, chers collÃ¨gues, VladimirÂ Poutine a dÃ©clarÃ© la guerre aux dÃ©mocraties europÃ©ennes. Cette guerre, il la mÃ¨ne depuis plus de dixÂ ans: cyberattaques, dÃ©sinformation, invasion militaireâ¦ Comment Poutine rÃ©ussit-il Ã  financer sa guerre? La rÃ©ponse est simple: les Ã©nergies fossiles. 140Â milliards d'euros, c'est le budget militaire de la Russie en guerre. 250Â milliardsÂ d'euros, ce sont les revenus que VladimirÂ Poutine tire de ses ventes de pÃ©trole et de gaz. C'est donc en s'en prenant au pÃ©trole et au gaz russe que nous priverons Poutine des moyens de gagner sa guerre.

Alors, que faire? D'abord, ne plus importer d'Ã©nergie russe: Nord Stream doit rester fermÃ© et les livraisons de GNL russe doivent cesser. Il faut cependant aller plus loin. En Europe comme en Inde, les Ã©nergies renouvelables sont les Ã©nergies de la libertÃ©. C'est en investissant Ã  l'Ã©chelle mondiale dans les Ã©nergies renouvelables que nous ferons reculer le pÃ©trole et le gaz russe. Notre pacte vert, c'est la kryptonite de VladimirÂ Poutine.

Â Â Krzysztof Hetman (PPE). â Panie PrzewodniczÄcy! Panie Komisarzu! Obecna sytuacja geopolityczna na Åwiecie sprawia, Å¼e kwestia bezpieczeÅstwa energetycznego staje siÄ jednym z kluczowych aspektÃ³w. W tym kontekÅcie przyspieszenie odejÅcia od rosyjskiego gazu i innych rosyjskich surowcÃ³w energetycznych staje siÄ jeszcze bardziej pilne. Konieczne jest zwiÄkszenie inwestycji w ÅºrÃ³dÅa odnawialne, ale rÃ³wnieÅ¼ w energiÄ atomowÄ, co pozwoli nam na dywersyfikacjÄ ÅºrÃ³deÅ energii i zwiÄkszenie naszej niezaleÅ¼noÅci energetycznej.

JednoczeÅnie musimy dokonaÄ rewizji Zielonego Åadu, ETS-u oraz zrezygnowaÄ z ETS2. W innym przypadku koszty energii caÅkowicie pozbawiÄ polski i europejski przemysÅ konkurencyjnoÅci. Aby sprostaÄ tym wyzwaniom, Polska podejmuje szereg dziaÅaÅ, jak chociaÅ¼by budowa terminalu LNG w GdaÅsku, ktÃ³ry umoÅ¼liwi odbiÃ³r gazu ziemnego z rÃ³Å¼nych kierunkÃ³w Åwiata i znaczÄco wzmocni bezpieczeÅstwo energetyczne Polski oraz regionu Europy Årodkowo-Wschodniej.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Sebastian Tynkkynen (ECR). â Mr President, every cent that we spend on Russian oil and natural gas results in the loss of Ukrainian lives. Last week, in a single night, Putin launched over 60Â missiles and 200Â drones towards Ukraine. These weapons are funded by Russia through the sale of natural resources.

If we want to stop Putin's imperialistic regime, we must increase our efforts to completely end our reliance on Russian energy. The only appropriate amount of Russian oil and natural gas in Europe is zero. We have to invest in our energy infrastructure and close every loophole that allows importing of refined Russian oil to Europe through third countries, like India and TÃ¼rkiye.

It is our responsibility to do everything we can so that the brave people of Ukraine can live their lives in peace. Let's cut our reliance on Russia!

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Emma Wiesner (Renew). â Mr President, in 2015, the Commission launched the plans for the energy union in order to make us more independent from Russian gas. Now, seven years later, we see the results from the major investment in LNG terminals and gas infrastructure and so on. And the result is very clear: at the start of this war, we were more dependent on Russian gas than ever. We import more, we use more gas and we import more gas from Russia.

We cannot afford to make the same mistakes again. So my question is to you, Commissioner: how will you make sure we don't repeat our mistakes? How will you make sure that the affordable energy action plan actually phases out gas and Russian gas for good?

We cannot afford to make the same mistakes again, and our new plans really need to make sure that we get rid of these dependencies, not dig us deeper into them.

Â 
Â Â 
Â Â 

(End of catch-the-eye procedure)

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Dan JÃ¸rgensen, Member of the Commission. â MrÂ President, and thank you, honourable Members, for an excellent debate. Many numbers have been shared, and I also want to share one with you: if we look at how much Europeans have paid Russia for energy since February 2022, the amount equals the cost price of 2Â 400 new F-35 fighter jets. This puts things into perspective. On one hand, you could say that we have put in place policies that are a huge success. I don't know of any other continent on the planet that could so fundamentally change its energy systems and dependencies, as we have done (45Â % of our gas came from Russia just three years ago). But on the other hand, you could also argue that, well, okay, we still import 13Â % of our gas from Russia, and this will obviously not stand. We are indirectly putting money into Putin's war chest.

Now the good news is, although this is a very sad situation, we do have the tools to do this. And the things that we need to do to become independent of gas from Russia are also the things we need to do to decarbonise our economy. It's also the things we need to do to become more competitive. Because it is wrong what some Members said here on this podium during this debate, that gas and fossils are the cheapest form of energy. That is simply not right. On the contrary, we are actually saving money every year by deploying more renewables and by being more energy efficient. The cheapest energy is the energy you don't use.

And things are going in the right direction. Last year, we deployed 78Â GW of new renewable energy in Europe. Last year was the first year ever when solar took over from coal in producing most electricity. So it is going in the right direction, but it's not going fast enough. That's why we put forward, recently, a plan on on bringing down the prices significantly in Europe â the affordable energy action plan â with a whole range of different measures that we will implement, together with the Member States, that will bring the speed up significantly whilst bringing the prices down, also significantly. I will also be putting forward very soon the roadmap that many of you have been asking for impatiently; I understand that. We are working hard on it. I can assure you that we are committed to delivering on this.

I want to finish here by repeating what President Ursula von der Leyen has said many times: there will be no backtracking on the green transition in Europe. I will add to that: if anything, there will be fastâtracking, because we want to do things even more efficiently, even faster than before.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â President. â The debate is closed.

Written Statements (Rule 178)

Â 
Â Â 
Â 		Â 

Â Â MirosÅawa Nykiel (PPE), na piÅmie. â Polska udowodniÅa, Å¼e odejÅcie od rosyjskich surowcÃ³w energetycznych jest nie tylko moÅ¼liwe, ale przede wszystkim konieczne dla bezpieczeÅstwa energetycznego. Nasz kraj caÅkowicie zaprzestaÅ importu gazu z Rosji, pokazujÄc, Å¼e odpowiedzialna polityka energetyczna to racja stanu w tych trudnych czasach.

Polska dziÅ opiera swoje bezpieczeÅstwo energetyczne na gazie sprowadzanym z Norwegii przez Baltic Pipe jak rÃ³wnieÅ¼ poprzez terminal w Åwinoujsciu z amerykaÅskim i katarskim LNG. KluczowÄ rolÄ odgrywajÄ magazyny gazu jakie posiadaliÅmy jeszcze przed wybuchem wojny.

Ponadto Polska poprzez interkonektory z naszymi sÄsiadami z UE oraz Ukrainy jest w stanie w kaÅ¼dej chwili przesyÅaÄ surowiec w obu kierunkach, co zapewnia nam elastycznoÅÄ w zarzadzaniu energiÄ i rÃ³wnoczeÅnie stabilizuje bezpieczeÅstwo dostaw.

DziÅ Polska jest przykÅadem dla Europy â pokazujemy, Å¼e moÅ¼na uniezaleÅ¼niÄ siÄ od rosyjskich surowcÃ³w i jednoczeÅnie zabezpieczyÄ dostawy energii. Czas, aby caÅa Unia poszÅa tÄ drogÄ. Nie moÅ¼emy zwalniaÄ tempa â bo bezpieczeÅstwo energetyczne to podstawa bezpieczeÅstwa w tak niepewnych czasach.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 		Â 

Â Â Petra Steger (PfE), schriftlich. â Sehr geehrte Damen und Herren,

Liebe Kollegen,

Wir diskutieren heute darÃ¼ber, wie wir uns Ã¶konomisch weiter von Russland abkoppeln kÃ¶nnen.

Liebe Kollegen, das ist gleich zweimal idiotisch:

Zum Einen ist ein Waffenstillstand in der Ukraine ist in greifbarer NÃ¤he,

die Verhandlungen in Riad laufen gut.

Sie mÃ¶gen das vielleicht nicht mitbekommen haben, unsere AuÃenbeauftragte, unsere AuÃenbeauftragte hat wirklich nichts getan um diesen Frieden mÃ¶glich zu machen.

Zum Anderen beziehen wir weiterhin Gas und Ãl aus Russland - wir zahlen nur viel mehr dafÃ¼r!

Drittstaaten kaufen in Russland billig ein und liefern teuer an uns weiter. Was fÃ¼r absoluter Wahnsinn!

Meine Damen und Herren, haben Sie denn den SCHUSS nicht gehÃ¶rt?

Sind Sie so gefangen in ihrer eigenen Welt, dass sie einfach die RealitÃ¤t ignorieren?

Europa ist keine Insel, wir werden auch in Zukunft mit Russland handeln - im beidseitigen Interesse.

Wenn Sie weiter auf Abschottung unseres Energiemarktes von russischem Gas und Ãl setzen, ruinieren Sie nicht nur europÃ¤ische Haushalte,

Sie machen Russland ausserdem zur permanenten Tankstelle Chinas.

Dann kÃ¶nnen die Chinesen ihre Wettbewerbsvorteile weiter ausbauen und wir kÃ¶nnen unserer Industrie beim Sterben zuschauen.

Nehmen Sie bitte endlich Vernunft an und beenden Sie diesen Wahnsinn.

Â 

16. EU Consumers Day: filling the gaps in protecting 440 million consumers in the EU (debate)
Video of the speeches
Â 

Â Â President. â The next item is the Council and Commission statements on EU Consumers Day: filling the gaps in protecting 440Â million consumers in the EU (2025/2597(RSP)).

Given the absence of the Council in the debate, I will give the floor directly to Commissioner JÃ¸rgensen.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Dan JÃ¸rgensen, Member of the Commission. â MrÂ President, honourable Members, thank you for having included this debateÂ on the agenda on the occasion of World Consumer Rights Day.

We can be proud in the EU: we enjoy some of the strongest consumer protection rules in the world. For example, on product safety. Our framework ensures fairness by building on strong legal principles, complemented with more detailed safeguards that guarantee transparency, remedies and cross-border enforcement.

However, consumers are still faced with many challenges and they count on us in Europe to deliver better than we do today. Consumer confidence in the economy remains below the historic average. Obstacles such as unjustified geoblocking still prevent consumers from fully accessing goods and services in the single market. Consumers are exposed to problematic marketing practices online, such as scams, dark patterns and addictive design features. Children are particularly vulnerable to these. Children can also be exposed to dangerous and unsafe products. This is especially the case as we see an increase of harmful products directly shipped to consumers from third countries. That is why it is so critical that together we continue advancing consumer rights in our Union.

Later this year, the Commission will put forward the consumer agenda for 2025â2030, defining the key EU priorities and actions in this area, with a view to better protecting consumers and to ensuring a level playing field for EU businesses. It will look at how to better protect vulnerable consumers and assess the need to update our legislation and strengthen its enforcement. Consumers play a key role in our social market economy, contributing to sustainable growth and to a more competitive single market. But we need to ensure that in cross-border situations, they do not face unfair discrimination and are protected when buying goods or services. That is why the consumer agenda will include an action plan on consumers in the single market. We aim to also help ensure a level playing field for businesses operating in the single market.

We want the consumer agenda to be a shared vision that makes a real difference in people's lives, and we look forward to engaging with Parliament and all other stakeholders.

Let me focus on three priorities.

First, digital fairness. Over the past years, the European Union has significantly strengthened its digital rulebook, adopting landmark laws such as the Digital Services Act, Digital Markets Act and Artificial Intelligence Act, while continuing to rely on the rules of the Audiovisual Media Services Directive. Complementing this framework with updated horizontal consumer protection rules is a missing piece of the puzzle to ensure a fair digital single market for all. Unethical techniques and commercial practices related to dark patterns, marketing by social media influencers, the addictive design of digital products and online profiling, especially when consumers' vulnerabilities are exploited for commercial purposes, are costing consumers time and money, and are distorting competition in the single market. The future Digital Fairness Act that Commissioner McGrath will propose in 2026 will fill these gaps. We will pay particular attention to the protection of children online â a priority that I know you all fully share.

The Digital Fairness Act will not duplicate or reopen matters already addressed in recently adopted legislation. We will stay focused on the gaps and seek simplification in the areas identified in the fitness check that was published in autumn last year, and ensure consistency with existing rules. We will also pay attention to views from companies to ensure that rules are easy to implement and very little burdensome.

The second main priority is enforcement. Our consumer acquis is solid, but we need to enforce it better. Recently coordinated enforcement actions by the Consumer Protection Cooperation Network against operators like Temu and Shein demonstrate how effective enforcement upholds consumers' rights and contributes to a level playing field. These enforcement actions show how the Digital Services Act and the CPC can reinforce each other.

The Commission's role of coordination is often crucial to ensure that Member States' authorities can effectively address EUâwide breaches of consumer law, but coordinated actions can take time and result only in voluntary commitments. We must be bolder, simpler and faster. We are therefore reflecting on ways to enhance and strengthen the existing cooperation system under the CPC Regulation.

Our third priority concerns product safety. In line with our recent e-commerce communication adopted onÂ 5Â February, we need to effectively work on the enforcement of product safety legislation. The new General Product Safety Regulation started to apply on 13Â December last year. It aims to ensure safety for all products, including those sold online. We are now focusing on our efforts on effective and predictable implementation and enforcement, fostering stronger cooperation between national market surveillance authorities and customs. We will organise the first product safety sweep before the summer. We will also support joint productâtesting activities with a focus on chemical safety. Moreover, we are committed to boosting cooperation with customs authorities and maintaining dialogue with China to enhance compliance with EU safety rules and ensure a level playing field within the single market.

Honourable Members, the Commission knows it can count on the support of this Parliament to advance consumers' rights. While I have outlined our longâterm objectives, the Commission also stands ready to facilitate the swift adaptation of two proposals currently in the interinstitutional negotiations, namely the Package Travel Directive and Alternative Dispute Resolution Directive. I am confident that together we are stronger and we will protect the rights of our 440Â million consumers.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Kamila Gasiuk-Pihowicz, w imieniu grupy PPE. â Panie PrzewodniczÄcy! Szanowni PaÅstwo! Dzisiaj jednym z najwiÄkszych wyzwaÅ dla ochrony 450 mln europejskich konsumentÃ³w jest handel internetowy. A w takich produktach uÅ¼ywanych na co dzieÅ nieprzestrzeganie przepisÃ³w bezpieczeÅstwa moÅ¼e mieÄ bardzo powaÅ¼ne konsekwencje dla zdrowia, a nawet dla Å¼ycia. Problem ten dotyczy w szczegÃ³lnoÅci towarÃ³w kupowanych przez azjatyckie platformy, takie jak Shein i Temu. Podam kilka przykÅadÃ³w: plastikowe zabawki zawierajÄce substancje zaburzajÄce gospodarkÄ hormonalnÄ 240 razy powyÅ¼ej dopuszczalnego limitu, nosideÅka dla niemowlÄt pÄkajÄce w szwach, zabawki rozpadajÄce siÄ w rÄkach, stwarzajÄce ryzyko zadÅawienia, kosmetyki uczulajÄce, a nawet zawierajÄce rakotwÃ³rcze skÅadniki, ubrania nasÄczone toksycznymi chemikaliami.

Europejscy przedsiÄbiorcy podejmujÄ olbrzymi wysiÅek i ponoszÄ znaczÄce koszty, aby dostarczaÄ konsumentom bezpieczne i jak najlepsze produkty. Dlatego, po pierwsze, wzywam KomisjÄ do utworzenia grupy zadaniowej ds. handlu elektronicznego w celu koordynacji dziaÅaÅ wszystkich sÅuÅ¼b na szczeblu Unii Europejskiej. A Komisji Å¼yczÄ zorganizowania silnej i skutecznej kampanii âBuy Europeanâ.

Â Â Alex Agius Saliba, f'isem il-grupp S&D. â Sur President, jekk irridu li l-proÄ¡ett Ewropew jibqaâ wieÄ§ed rilevanti rridu nkunu Äerti li s-suq Ewropew jaÄ§dem bâmod Ä¡ust favur il-konsumaturi kollha.

Ma jistax ikun li r-riÄ¡iditÃ  tas-suq Ewropew twassal biex fi Stati Membri Å¼gÄ§ar u gÅ¼ejjer bÄ§al Malta nispiÄÄaw bâsuq uniku li jaÄ§dem favur ftit importaturi l-kbar u kontra l-Ä¡id komuni taÄ-Äittadini tagÄ§na.

Â Â Jorge MartÃ­n FrÃ­as, en nombre del Grupo PfE. â SeÃ±or presidente, seÃ±orÃ­as, la ComisiÃ³n asume mÃ¡s y mÃ¡s competencias que no le corresponden a la vez que no cumple con sus funciones mÃ¡s bÃ¡sicas: aquellas que protegen al consumidor y garantizan la libertad de mercado.

A dÃ­a de hoy, la ComisiÃ³n no ha hecho nada para impedir que China engaÃ±e a los consumidores con la copia del marcado de conformidad europea. Ya llevamos veinte aÃ±os, Â¡veinte aÃ±os! Eso sÃ­, mientras la ComisiÃ³n se dedica a regular y asfixiar a nuestra industria y nuestras empresas, China inunda el mercado Ãºnico con productos que no cumplen los estÃ¡ndares europeos y que, en muchos casos, suponen un peligro para el consumidor sin que este lo sepa; casi un 40Â %, segÃºn datos de la propia ComisiÃ³n.

Mientras esa promesa de simplificaciÃ³n de burocracia sigue sin llegar âporque no saben cÃ³mo de grande es el monstruo que han creadoâ nuestros competidores se burlan de nosotros ante la clara dejaciÃ³n de funciones. La ComisiÃ³n debe registrar sin mÃ¡s demora el logotipo de conformidad europea y ejercer de una vez un liderazgo que acabe con la competencia desleal y con los riesgos para los consumidores.

Â Â 
Â 

Â Â StÃ©phanie Yon-Courtin, au nom du groupe Renew. â Monsieur le PrÃ©sident, Monsieur le Commissaire, chers collÃ¨gues, aujourd'hui, 440Â millions de consommateurs europÃ©ens comptent sur nous pour garantir leurs droits dans un marchÃ© en pleine mutation. Chaque jour, Temu, Shein et d'autres inondent le marchÃ© de produits peu chers, souvent non sÃ©curisÃ©s, non conformes ou contrefaits, et dangereux.

Pendant ce temps, sur TikTok, on trouve des apprentis influenceurs, parfois des adolescents depuis leur chambre, qui vous vendent des placements financiers ou de la chirurgie esthÃ©tique. Alors que la publicitÃ© tÃ©lÃ©visÃ©e est encadrÃ©e, cette influence en ligne, parfois trÃ¨s risquÃ©e, Ã©chappe Ã  tout contrÃ´le.

En parallÃ¨le, les grandes plateformes perfectionnent des techniques de manipulation, des interfaces trompeuses, comme les faux comptes Ã  rebours, qui crÃ©ent un sentiment d'urgence pour pousser Ã  l'achat, ou encore le dÃ©filement infini, conÃ§u pour garder nos enfants et nous-mÃªmes scotchÃ©s Ã  l'Ã©cran. RÃ©sultat: nos choix ne sont plus les nÃ´tres.

L'Europe a dÃ©jÃ  posÃ© des rÃ¨gles, mais elles doivent Ãªtre appliquÃ©es efficacement et il faut aller plus loin: protÃ©ger les consommateurs partout, tout le temps, dans un marchÃ© numÃ©rique qui ne connaÃ®t pas de frontiÃ¨res. La protection des consommateurs est une avancÃ©e concrÃ¨te de l'Europe, que nous ne voyons mÃªme plus, tant que nous la tenons pour acquise. Ne laissons pas le numÃ©rique crÃ©er une zone de non-droit ni des consommateurs de seconde zone. Nous devons donc avancer sur une lÃ©gislation ambitieuse pour l'Ã©quitÃ© numÃ©rique, qui protÃ¨ge les consommateurs dans l'environnement numÃ©rique du XXIáµ siÃ¨cle.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Anna Cavazzini, im Namen der Verts/ALE-Fraktion. â Herr PrÃ¤sident, liebe Kolleginnen und Kollegen! Wir wissen es alle: In der EuropÃ¤ischen Union haben wir die hÃ¶chsten Verbraucherschutzstandards weltweit, egal, ob wir in Tschechien, Belgien oder in Italien einkaufen. Diese hohen Verbraucherschutzstandards mÃ¼ssen wir auch im OnlineâHandel durchsetzen; viele Kolleginnen und Kollegen haben das heute schon gesagt. Das ist die groÃe Aufgabe dieser Legislaturperiode.

Immer mehr Billigprodukte kommen direkt aus Drittstaaten in unsere Wohnzimmer, in unsere Kinderzimmer; und hier werden die Produkte zum Sicherheitsrisiko. Eine Zahl vielleicht mal: 95Â % der bei Temu gekauften Spielzeuge verstoÃen gegen unsere Sicherheitsvorschriften. Daher appelliere ich an die MitgliedstaatenÂ â der Rat ist heute nicht daÂ â, endlich mit uns in die Verhandlungen zur Zollreform zu gehen, um diesen Paket-Tsunami endlich handhaben zu kÃ¶nnen.

Ich fordere die Kommission auf, dem Verbraucherschutz endlich ein wirkliches Update fÃ¼r das digitale Zeitalter zu geben. Dazu gehÃ¶ren: die konsequente Umsetzung des DSA, das Ende der Manipulation durch einen Digital Fairness Act, eine effizientere MarktÃ¼berwachung und endlich mehr Enforcement bei der Kommission und mehr Verantwortung fÃ¼r die OnlineâMarktplÃ¤tze, wenn sie ihren Auflagen nicht nachkommen. Dann sind endlich Verbraucherinnen und Verbraucher auch im OnlineâShopping sicher.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Leila Chaibi, au nom du groupe The Left. â Monsieur le PrÃ©sident, chers collÃ¨gues, Monsieur le Commissaire, bientÃ´t, c'est l'anniversaire de ma fille et de mon neveu. Alors, pour fÃªter cela, j'ai fait des achats sur internet. Mon neveu est fan de manga, alors je lui ai achetÃ© de petites figurines de OneÂ Piece. Ma fille, en ce moment, adore les animaux. J'ai trouvÃ© plein de jouets de bain en forme de vache et d'hippopotame. Pour la dÃ©co, j'ai commandÃ© plein de ballons de baudruche.

Je suis loin de me douter que je suis sur le point de tous nous empoisonner, ma fille, mon neveu et moi. Ces objets viennent de plateformes comme Amazon, Shein et Temu, qui Ã©chappent aux rÃ©glementations europÃ©ennes. En gonflant ces ballons de baudruche, j'avalerai des substances cancÃ©rigÃ¨nes nommÃ©es nitrosamines; en jouant avec leurs cadeaux tout mignons, tout colorÃ©s, mon neveu et ma fille respireront du phtalate, un produit chimique qui altÃ¨re le systÃ¨me hormonal des enfants et leur dÃ©veloppement cÃ©rÃ©bral.

Si nous tombons malades, personne n'est responsable, parce que ces vendeurs se trouvent hors de l'Union europÃ©enne. Il faut mettre un terme Ã  cette impunitÃ©. Les plateformes doivent Ãªtre responsables de ce qu'elles vendent. Ces objets ont leur place Ã  la poubelle, pas entre les mains de nos enfants (l'oratrice prÃ©sente quelques exemples de jouets).

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â President. â (addressing the previous speaker) I want to remind you that we cannot show items when we have the floor.

Â 
Â Â 
Â

Â Â Laura BallarÃ­n Cereza (S&D). â SeÃ±or presidente, con ocasiÃ³n del DÃ­a Mundial de los Derechos del Consumidor, tenemos un debate en esta CÃ¡mara para celebrar todo lo que hemos avanzado en Europa para proteger a nuestros consumidores como en ninguna otra regiÃ³n del mundo: el cargador universal, el derecho a reparar, la seguridad de los productos que vienen de fuera de Europa o los derechos digitales frente a las grandes plataformas. Pero todavÃ­a nos queda mucho mÃ¡s por hacer. Â¿QuiÃ©n no ha sufrido una cancelaciÃ³n de vuelo en el Ãºltimo minuto sin obtener ninguna compensaciÃ³n? Este Parlamento seguirÃ¡ trabajando para acabar con las estafas, los diseÃ±os adictivos y las malas prÃ¡cticas en la futura Ley de Equidad Digital que saludamos y que la ComisiÃ³n nos ha presentado, y para tener tambiÃ©n un buen acuerdo en la resoluciÃ³n alternativa de litigios para dar mÃ¡s herramientas a los consumidores para defender sus derechos.

On this World Consumer Rights Day, Europe faces two big challenges due to the current geopolitical context. First, Europe is not the land of the rule of the strongest, but the place in which real laws are made to defend our citizens and consumers. But these laws must be enforced if we really want to protect the more vulnerable users, like minors or women, from online hate and cyberbullying, and also protect our democracies from disinformation. In front of any attempt at blackmail, the EU needs to choose its side. Either we defend the strongest â the big tech oligarchs who hate women, migrants and European democracy â or we protect European consumer rights. For me, the choice is clear.

Second, in the current context of trade wars, European consumers have the opportunity to defend European businesses by choosing products made in Europe. We are a market, or 440Â million consumers. This is our strength, our leverage. Let's use it. So think global, buy local.

Â 
Â Â 
Â

TÃ¡ sÃºil aige go mbeidh an carr sin ar chaighdeÃ¡n maith. NÃ­ meicneoir Ã© agus dÃ¡ bhrÃ­ sin tÃ¡ muinÃ­n aige go ndÃ©anfaidh caighdeÃ¡in an Aontais Ã© a chosaint mar thomhaltÃ³ir. NÃ­l uaidh ach praghas cÃ³ir, agus nÃ­os tÃ¡bhachtaÃ­, carr sÃ¡bhÃ¡ilte. Ach is minic go gcuirtear an dubh ina gheal ar dhaoine maidir le lÃ­on na gcilimÃ©adar atÃ¡ curtha de ag an gcarr. DÃ©anaimis gnÃ­omh anois. TÃ¡ feidhmiÃº an mhargaidh aonair ag brath air. Go raibh mÃ­le maith agaibh.

Â Â

Â Â Kim Van Sparrentak (Verts/ALE). â MrÂ President, today we celebrate consumer protection in the EU. But when I look at addictive apps like TikTok, Tinder, Instagram and Netflix, I don't see so much to celebrate. Too many platforms and websites manipulate consumers into spending time and attention there. This is one of the challenges of our time. Nearly everyone â children, youngsters, students, adults and grandparents alike â feels they have lost control over their time spent online.

More than a year ago, Parliament was very clear: stop manipulative tricks to glue us to our screens in the Digital Fairness Act and ban the most harmful features, like the autoplay of videos and the endless scroll. But there are plenty of rumours that the Commission will actually go soft on US tech companies to please Trump. Surely it's us determining our own rules, not the tech oligarchy across the ocean? We should take control of our own time, with our own rules, on our own ethical design of apps. Commissioner, I expect you to deliver on this.

(The speaker agreed to take a blue-card question)

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Lukas Sieper (NI), blue-card question. â My question to you, colleague, would be: in times where the tech oligarchs, as you said, develop more and more into the enemies of democracy, should we maybe think about taking away these companies on European soil from them?

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Kim Van Sparrentak (Verts/ALE), blue-card answer. â Thank you for this question. I think there are very important decisions to be made around what we do with these companies. I think that they are very harmful. I think that these companies are a danger to our democracy and to our health and to our societies as a whole.

That's why I think it is very important that we start doing a cost-benefit analysis on what these companies actually bring to the European Union and what harm they bring. If the decision is that they are more harmful than actually beneficial, then maybe they don't belong in the European Union.

Â 
Â Â 
Â

Â Â Christel Schaldemose (S&D). â Hr. Formand! KommissÃ¦r! Europa er verdens bedste sted at vÃ¦re forbruger. Det skyldes Ã¥rtiers kamp for stÃ¦rke rettigheder og god beskyttelse. Men vi mÃ¥ ikke hvile pÃ¥ laurbÃ¦rrene. Selvom der er meget at vÃ¦re stolte af, sÃ¥ mÃ¥ vi aldrig tage vores resultater for givet. Forbrugerbeskyttelse er en konstant kamp, og hver dag opstÃ¥r nye trusler, der skal hÃ¥ndteres. Og lige nu svigter vi isÃ¦r de yngste af vores forbrugere, nÃ¥r de er online. De mÃ¸der ulovligt indhold, de bliver manipuleret, de bliver kommercielt udnyttet, og de bliver afhÃ¦ngige. Techgiganterne har haft deres chance for at tage ansvar. De ved, at deres services gÃ¸r skade pÃ¥ de unge mennesker, og alligevel fortsÃ¦tter de. Derfor skal vi handle. Vi skal sikre, at de unge ikke bliver en vare, som udnyttes for profit. SÃ¥ derfor skal vi ikke stoppe her. Vi skal fortsat kÃ¦mpe for at fÃ¥ hÃ¥ndhÃ¦vet de regler, vi har, og om nÃ¸dvendigt ogsÃ¥ fÃ¥ flere regler, sÃ¥ vi kan beskytte de alleryngste forbrugere. Vi skal fortsat kÃ¦mpe for de europÃ¦iske forbrugerrettigheder ogsÃ¥ i en verden, som stiller andre krav til os. Det handler om tryghed for alle vores 440 millioner forbrugere.

Â Â Tomislav Sokol (PPE). â PoÅ¡tovani potpredsjedniÄe, povjereniÄe, kolegice i kolege, uvoÄenje jedinstvenog punjaÄa, donoÅ¡enje novih pravila o oznaÄavanju meda, rjeÅ¡avanje problema minusa na raÄunima ili pak uvoÄenje prava na popravak proizvoda, sve su to inicijative na kojima smo radili da bismo unaprijedili poloÅ¾aj potroÅ¡aÄa i dodatno ih zaÅ¡titili na zajedniÄkom trÅ¾iÅ¡tu.

U novom mandatu suoÄavamo se s bitno razliÄitim geopolitiÄkim okolnostima koje utjeÄu i na poziciju potroÅ¡aÄa. Generirana inflacija smanjuje kupovnu moÄ graÄana, a velike meÄunarodne kompanije sve ÄeÅ¡Äe se sluÅ¾e nepoÅ¡tenim poslovnim praksama. Jedan od velikih problema koji je prisutan u nekoliko drÅ¾ava Älanica, ukljuÄujuÄi Hrvatsku, tiÄu se istih proizvoda koji se prodaju po bitno drugaÄijim cijenama u razliÄitim drÅ¾avama Älanicama. Postoje proizvodi koji se u jednoj drÅ¾avi prodaju po dva do tri puta viÅ¡oj cijeni nego u istom trgovaÄkom lancu u susjednoj drÅ¾avi Älanici, a najveÄi je problem s prehrambenim proizvodima. Takvom ponaÅ¡anju treba stati na kraj. Zato pozivam Europsku komisiju da se u strategiji za potroÅ¡aÄe posebno pozabavi ovim problemom i Å¡to prije revidira Direktivu o nepoÅ¡tenoj poslovnoj praksi.

U Europi ne smiju postojati potroÅ¡aÄi prvog i drugog reda.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Pierfrancesco Maran (S&D). â Signor Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, in occasione della Giornata dei diritti dei consumatori, vorrei portare all'attenzione di quest'Aula un tema caro alle associazioni dei consumatori, che ci ricorda il nostro ruolo in difesa dei consumatori anche in soggetti ben regolati e a forte concorrenza, come quello delle assicurazioni auto.

Il costo delle assicurazioni sta crescendo in Europa: infatti, sono aumentate di quasi il 20% nell'ultimo triennio. Questo aumento colpisce tutti ma in particolar modo i giovani neopatentati, che spesso hanno anche un potere di acquisto minore.

Il mondo assicurativo lo spiega con l'inflazione e l'incremento dei costi di riparazione che incidono, sÃ¬, ma, dati alla mano, non nella misura degli aumenti delle polizze: il sospetto Ã¨ che queste due cause siano anche la scusa per aumentare i prezzi a discapito dei consumatori.

Caro Commissario, la Commissione europea, garante del mercato interno, deve vigilare affinchÃ© nel 2025 il mercato riporti i prezzi sotto controllo. Ce lo chiedono le associazioni dei consumatori ma, soprattutto, Ã¨ un'esigenza delle famiglie europee.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Biljana Borzan (S&D). â PoÅ¡tovani predsjedavajuÄi, sretan dan potroÅ¡aÄa svim naÅ¡im graÄanima! Ili moÅ¾da ne baÅ¡ svim? Jer bolje je biti potroÅ¡aÄ u jednom dijelu Europske unije nego u drugom.

Negdje su rasprodaje stvarne, a drugdje su samo marketinÅ¡ki trik. Neki lako kupuju preko interneta, a za vaÅ¡u zemlju moÅ¾da nema dostave. Negdje je cijena telefonskog poziva razumna, a vama je preskupa jer vas je teleoperater stavio u isti koÅ¡ s Marokom ili Novim Zelandom. PotroÅ¡aÄka prava uvijek zavrÅ¡avaju na istoj granici, onoj izmeÄu zapadne i istoÄne Europe. Svjedoci smo najviÅ¡eg rasta cijena osnovnih proizvoda upravo u zemljama s najniÅ¾im primanjima. Pola kile tjestenine je dva puta skuplje u Hrvatskoj nego u Francuskoj. Pasta za zube tri puta skuplja u Bugarskoj nego u NjemaÄkoj. Äesto nam se kaÅ¾e, morate bolje upoznati svoja prava. Kao da je naÅ¡a krivnja Å¡to nitko na trÅ¾iÅ¡tu ne radi u skladu s njima. Å to vrijedi imati pravo na povrat novca ako ga trgovac ne Å¾eli vratiti. PotroÅ¡aÄka prava ne smiju biti mrtvo slovo na papiru.

Samo efikasan nadzor i stroge kazne mogu donijeti promjenu.

Â 
Â Â 
Â Â 

Catch-the-eye procedure

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Nina Carberry (PPE). â MrÂ President, the European single market has been the foundation of our economic success, connecting 450 million consumers across 27 countries. But let's be honest: it's not yet finished. And by leaving it unfinished, we are letting down European consumers and businesses alike.

The biggest gap is services: 70Â % of the EU economy. Yet complex regulations and red tape make it tough for companies, especially SMEs, to expand across borders. Then there's the Capital Markets Union. We currently rely too much on banks for funding. We need to let savings and investments flow more freely across Europe. And let's not forget digital and energy markets â still fragmented. The solution for this? Simplified EUâwide rules.

And the only final point: European consumers also expect safe and traceable products, particularly in agriâfood goods. But when imports fail, we must meet standards, and we must react swiftly to protect businesses, farmers and consumers alike.

So 30 years after the single market was born, it's time to finish what we started.

Â 
Â Â 
Â Â 

SÄDI VADA: ROBERTS ZÄªLE
PriekÅ¡sÄdÄtÄja vietnieks

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Lukas Sieper (NI). â Herr PrÃ¤sident, werte Menschen Europas! Herr Kommissar, ich gebe Ihnen einen Moment. Ich bin Jurist, ich habe Jura studiert, und eine der ersten Stellen, an denen ich mich ernsthaft mit der EuropÃ¤ischen Union auseinandersetzen musste, war das Verbraucherschutzrecht. Das Verbraucherschutzrecht ist in der deutschen Juristenausbildung ein sehr beliebtes Thema. Wir mÃ¼ssen uns alle sehr genau damit befassen, und dementsprechend hat es aber auch eine Bedeutung: Es ist eine der Grundlagen dieser Union, die Idee, dass in unserem Binnenmarkt, in diesem Raum des freien Handels, die Menschen, die an der ungeschÃ¼tztesten Stelle des Marktes stehen, durch die Union den Schutz erhalten, um eine faire Position zu bekommen.

Und dementsprechend mÃ¶chte ich diesen EU Consumers Day nutzen, um Ihnen diese Geschichte mitzugeben. Bitte denken Sie immer daran, dass das Verbraucherschutzrecht der EuropÃ¤ischen Union einer der Bereiche ist, in dem unsere BÃ¼rgerinnen und BÃ¼rger am allermeisten berÃ¼hrt werden.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Julien Sanchez (PfE). â Monsieur le PrÃ©sident, la Commission ose aujourd'hui lancer un dÃ©bat sur la protection des consommateurs. Quelques vÃ©ritÃ©s doivent donc Ãªtre rappelÃ©es. D'abord, sans votre marchÃ© europÃ©en de l'Ã©nergie, les FranÃ§ais pourraient avoir le nuclÃ©aire, EDF et l'Ã©lectricitÃ© la moins chÃ¨re, et Ãªtre un eldorado pour les entreprises en Europe. Au lieu de cela, vous spoliez les FranÃ§ais, avec la complicitÃ© de Macron.

Ensuite, n'est-ce pas l'Union europÃ©enne qui, via son accord avec le Mercosur, veut importer de la viande nourrie aux hormones de croissance et aux antibiotiques? Vous osez nous faire croire qu'il y aura des contrÃ´les? Je serais curieux d'aller faire des contrÃ´les-surprises moi-mÃªme au BrÃ©sil, avec des organismes indÃ©pendants. En voulant mettre cela dans nos assiettes, quelle lÃ©gitimitÃ© avez-vous pour parler de protection des consommateurs? Nous continuerons Ã  nous y opposer fermement, pour notre souverainetÃ© alimentaire, notre santÃ© publique et nos agriculteurs.

N'est-ce pas aussi vous qui voulez interdire l'acquisition de voitures neuves Ã©mettant du CO2 Ã  compter deÂ 2035, plombant la filiÃ¨re automobile europÃ©enne, faisant un beau cadeau Ã  nos concurrents et sacrifiant nos consommateurs au nom d'idÃ©ologies? Je n'oublie pas le pacte vert ni les contrats de Pfizer nÃ©gociÃ©s par SMS.

Alors, de grÃ¢ce, cessez de nous faire perdre notre temps ici avec des dÃ©bats cyniques sur la protection des consommateurs. Parlez plutÃ´t de ce que vous connaissez mieux: l'opacitÃ©, les taxes, les interdictions dÃ©lirantes et les contraintes ubuesques. C'est vous qui Ãªtes les premiers spoliateurs des consommateurs; mais les peuples europÃ©ens se rÃ©veillent.

Â 
Â Â 
Â Â 

(BrÄ«vÄ mikrofona uzstÄÅ¡anos beigas.)

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Dan JÃ¸rgensen, Member of the Commission. â MrÂ President, thank you, honourable Members, for an interesting debate. I'm sure you, like me, often experience when speaking to citizens in our Member States that sometimes they feel that maybe what we do in Brussels and Strasbourg seems a little bit far away from their everyday lives, because they hear about directives and regulations and acquis communautaire. But what we've discussed here today, that is exactly about the everyday living of ordinary citizens in our community.

The consumer protection rights that we have in the European Union, the legislation that we've made, protect citizens every day. We all consume from the moment we get up in the morning until we go to bed at night. Whether we want it or not, we are part of an internal market where we consume products that are produced under pretty strict rules. I say 'pretty' because we're not there yet. We've come a far way â Christel Schaledemose and others spoke about that â but we need to go even further. And as the world changes, and more become part of an online marketplace, then also, of course, our regulation needs to follow that development.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â PriekÅ¡sÄdÄtÄjs. â Debates ir slÄgtas.

Â 

17. Debate on cases of breaches of human rights, democracy and the rule of law (debate)

17.1. Democracy and human rights in Thailand, notably the lese-majesty law and the deportation of Uyghur refugees
Video of the speeches
Â 

Â Â PriekÅ¡sÄdÄtÄjs. â NÄkamais darba kÄrtÄ«bas punkts ir debates par seÅ¡iem rezolÅ«ciju priekÅ¡likumiem par demokrÄtiju un cilvÄktiesÄ«bÄm TaizemÄ, jo Ä«paÅ¡i likumu par valsts zaimoÅ¡anu un uiguru bÄgÄ¼u deportÄÅ¡anu (2025/2594(RSP)).

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Daniel Attard, author. â MrÂ President, Thailand is a country I have visited, a place of rich culture and resilience. We see Thailand as a valued partner of the European Union and today I am not here to preach, but to strengthen that partnership â one which is rooted in democracy, human rights and mutual respect. Yet, real partnerships require honesty, and we must be honest about our deep concern over the prosecution of Thai parliamentarians, opposition figures and human rights defenders under the lese-majesty law.

Peaceful democratic discourse should never be a crime. Their voices should be heard, not silenced. Democracy thrives when dissent is debated, not punished. For this reason, we strongly urge the Thai authorities to grant an amnesty to those who are prosecuted or imprisoned for the peaceful exercise of their rights.

We are also concerned about the deportation of Uyghur asylum seekers and the reports of mistreatment they face. By strengthening its refugee protection, Thailand has an opportunity to lead by example.

Our resolution today is not about condemnation. It's about engagement. Thailand has made progress and we recognise that. But meaningful reforms, particularly to the lese-majesty law, must continue to ensure fundamental freedoms are upheld. We urge the Thai Government to take concrete steps forward towards greater democracy, political inclusivity and human rights protections.

The free trade agreement negotiation is a process that can bring prosperity anchored in shared values. A thriving, open democracy strengthens Thailand's global position and reassures its partners that economic and political progress can go hand in hand.

To our Thai friends, we stand ready to work with you, not against you. This is not just about an EU resolution. It is about the future of Thai democracy and the fundamental rights of its people. Let us use this moment not as a division, but as an opportunity â one to engage, to reform and to move forward together.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Engin Eroglu, Verfasser. â Herr PrÃ¤sident, sehr geehrter Herr Kommissar! Ãber was reden wir heute? Es sind 40Â Menschen aus Thailand nach China abgeschoben. Und das eigentlich Tragische ist, dass wir heute darÃ¼ber diskutieren, denn Ã¼ber die Abschiebung wissen wir schon mehrere Wochen Bescheid. Und wir hatten mit einer Gruppe von Abgeordneten schon in der letzten Plenarwoche zu StraÃburg gefordert, dass wir diese Aussprache haben, bevor die Menschen von Thailand nach China abgeschoben werden. 40Â Menschen, dieÂ â und das ist das eigentlich Tragische daranÂ â auch noch von anderen Staaten, nicht-europÃ¤ischen Staaten, aufgenommen werden sollten. Wir haben glaubhafte Berichte gesehen, dass andere LÃ¤nder gesagt haben: Wir nehmen diese 40Â Menschen auf, schiebt sie nicht zurÃ¼ck nach China.

Und wir verschieben diese Debatte, weil eine Delegation des EuropÃ¤ischen Parlaments, Herr VizeprÃ¤sidentÂ â die ja das PrÃ¤sidium, in dem Sie sitzen, genehmigt hatÂ â, nach Thailand fÃ¤hrt. Also, wenn das nicht eine TragÃ¶die ist und wenn wir uns dafÃ¼r nicht schÃ¤men mÃ¼ssen, dann weiÃ ich auch nicht, was wir richtig und falsch machen. Und deswegen, bitte, wenn das wiederkommt: Das darf nicht wieder passieren.

Und das andere ist: Das sind 40Â Menschen, die gehÃ¶ren einer Minderheit an, den Uiguren, und glauben Sie mir, die kommen dort nicht im Guten an, sondern die werden dort wahrscheinlich genauso wie die anderen Uiguren sehr schlimme Repressalien erleben mÃ¼ssen, und das darf nicht sein.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Erik Marquardt, Verfasser. â Herr PrÃ¤sident, Herr Kommissar! Ich mÃ¶chte auch zuallererst klar machen: Wir wollen eine gute Partnerschaft mit Thailand, aber zu einer guten Partnerschaft gehÃ¶rt eben auch ein gemeinsames Wertefundament. Deswegen ist diese EntschlieÃung auch ein Anlass, unsere Besorgnis auszudrÃ¼cken darÃ¼ber, dass dieses gemeinsame Wertefundament immer weiter zu erodieren droht.

Es ist nicht akzeptabel, wenn in Thailand Oppositionspolitiker unter Druck gesetzt werden, wenn ihnen angedroht wird, dass sie ihr Leben lang nicht mehr Politik ausÃ¼ben kÃ¶nnen, nur weil sie andere Meinungen haben, wenn die Meinungsfreiheit beschrÃ¤nkt wird. Es ist auch nicht akzeptabel, dass in den letzten Jahren 2000Â Menschenrechtsverteidigerinnen und Menschenrechtsverteidiger, Journalistinnen und Journalisten strafrechtlich verfolgt wurden. NatÃ¼rlich ist es auch nicht akzeptabel, dass Uiguren nach China deportiert werden, wo ihnen willkÃ¼rliche Inhaftierung, aber eben auch Folter droht.

Wir erwarten, dass die Rechte von Minderheiten wie der Uiguren geschÃ¼tzt werden. Und in der Tat erwarte ich auch vom Parlament und von der Delegation des Parlaments, dass sie sich fÃ¼r diese Rechte ganz aktiv auch bei diesen Delegationsreisen einsetzt. Ich glaube, es ist deswegen wichtig, dass wir diese EntschlieÃung beschlieÃen, dass wir mit groÃer Geschlossenheit, aber auch Entschlossenheit, Verbesserungen einfordern, diese aber auch gemeinsam mit den thailÃ¤ndischen BehÃ¶rden versuchen zu erringen.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Susanna Ceccardi, autrice. â Signor Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, l'11 settembre 2018 la dottoressa Abbas, medico in pensione, Ã¨ stata prelevata con la forza; un arresto che sembra legato alle critiche pubbliche espresse da sua sorella, meno di una settimana prima, durante un evento presso un think tank statunitense, in cui denunciava la repressione degli uiguri.

Da allora la sua famiglia non ha piÃ¹ avuto notizie di lei. Il 27 febbraio 2020 almeno 40 uiguri sono stati rimpatriati dalla Thailandia in Cina, un'azione che desta profonda preoccupazione e che viola gli standard internazionali assunti dal Paese.

In Cina gli uiguri affrontano detenzioni arbitrarie, condizioni di lavoro forzato e restrizioni alle libertÃ  fondamentali. Inoltre, il governo cinese sta estendendo la sua influenza ben oltre i propri confini, esercitando pressioni su altri Stati affinchÃ© collaborino al rimpatrio forzato di dissidenti e minoranze perseguitate.

L'Unione europea deve andare oltre le semplici dichiarazioni di condanna ma Ã¨ necessario adottare misure concrete, una maggiore trasparenza nelle filiere produttive e restrizioni commerciali per i prodotti legati allo sfruttamento del lavoro forzato, che danneggiano le nostre imprese, sottoposte a standard rigidi e a regolamentazioni spesso folli, applicate da burocrati inflessibili.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Miriam Lexmann, on behalf of the PPE Group. â MrÂ President, colleagues, Commissioner, just in the recent years, the Chinese Communist Party has built an Orwellian state that has introduced mass surveillance, forced labour and placed over a million Uyghurs in a concentration camp.

This is the reason why many Uyghurs escape the brutal regime while placing their trust in the hands of the international community. Thailand has completely failed these victims and violated international guarantees of their dignity and rights. They were kept in inhumane conditions before being sent back to China, where they face the risk of torture and imprisonment.

Dear colleagues, this is yet another proof of why we have to rethink our relations with China, ensuring a value-based and consistent approach towards the Chinese Communist Party. Only such a principled foreign policy can protect human rights and our economic interests. Thailand and our partner countries have to decide where they want to stand.

Â 
Â Â 
Â Â 

BrÄ«vais mikrofons

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Lukas Sieper (NI). â MrÂ President, dearÂ people of Europe, Thailand has just deported Uyghurs back to China.

Let's not sugarcoat it â and I thank you, colleague, that you did not. These people are being sent to concentration camps - not prisons, not re-education centres. Concentration camps.

And no, as far as we know, there are no gas chambers, but there is torture, there is forced sterilisation, there is forced marriage and the complete eradication of their culture.

This is not a political exaggeration. This is not based on rumours. This is based on facts reported by Der Spiegel, by the Fraunhofer Institute and by other renowned institutions.

Europe knows the horrors of concentration camps. We know what happens when the world stays silent in the face of such madness. We must be clear: no country in the world shall send Uyghurs back to China. We in this House, across all political divides, must agree on one thing: when people are thrown into concentration camps, we stand up. Because if we don't, who will?

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â OndÅej DostÃ¡l (NI). â MrÂ President, dear colleagues, I had an opportunity to be a part of a European Parliament mission to Thailand, and we tried to build friendly relations, which we need because we are in danger of trade wars and Thailand is and can be a valuable partner to the European Union.

Firstly, I see it as wrong to 'strongly condemn' somebody who should be our partner â and 'strongly condemn' is the wording of the declaration â in case we are not a court of law and we cannot assess what actually happens there.

The position of Thailand is that the Uyghurs are safe. There are safeguards in China that are put in place. The opinions of our colleagues are different, but we should leave it to the court to resolve these things.

Another issue is the critique of internal laws like the criminal lese-majesty law. Thailand has a kingdom for 800 years, and it is not very respectful from us to intervene in their domestic affairs. Please reconsider.

Â 
Â Â 
Â Â 

(BrÄ«vÄ mikrofona uzstÄÅ¡anos beigas.)

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Dan JÃ¸rgensen, Member of the Commission. â MrÂ President, honourable Members of the European Parliament, Thailand is among the EU's longestâstanding partners in Asia. In 2022, we signed the partnership and cooperation agreement. And in 2023, free trade agreement negotiations resumed. The EU and Thailand have started a successful cooperation on security issues, including maritime security, the law of the sea and the fight against organised crime. The partnership and cooperation agreement with Thailand marks an important step towards strengthening bilateral relations based on shared universal values, such as democracy and human rights.

Thailand is also an important partner in the region within the Association of Southeast Asian Nations, and multilaterally within the United Nations, where Thailand recently supported the EUâsponsored resolution on a comprehensive, just and lasting peace in Ukraine.

In 2023, Thailand successfully held parliamentarian elections, which resulted in a peaceful transfer of power to a new civilian government for the first time since the 2014 coup. It is important that the political space in Thailand remains open, and that restored political pluralism and fundamental freedoms are protected.

Regarding the human rights right situation, while there has been some progress, challenges remain in some areas. Sedition and computer crime charges are used to limit free speech. Failure to comply with the royal defamation legal framework was at the origin of the motion for the dissolution of the main opposition party. We hope that Thailand will refrain from imposing disproportionate sentences in the lese-majesty cases, especially on children and young people.

On 27Â February 2025, Thailand proceeded with the deportation of 40 Uyghur refugees to China. The EU deeply regrets the decision by the Thai Government, taken in violation of the principles of non-refoulement, as well as of Thailand's obligation under national and international law. It is detrimental to Thailand's international image. We have expressed our serious concerns directly to the Ministry of Foreign Affairs in Bangkok.

We also noted positive developments on the human rights situation in Thailand, like the recently adopted Marriage Equality Act, which enshrines into law the equal recognition of relationships for sameâsex couples. The EU welcomes this step. Thailand became the first Southeast Asian country to recognise sameâsex unions. Thailand was also elected a member of the Human Rights Council for 2025 and 2026, and continues its efforts to ratify the core human rights instruments. In 2024, Thailand ratified the International Convention for the Protection of All Persons from Enforced Disappearances and started enforcing the Prevention and Suppression of Torture and Enforced Disappearance Act.

The EU supports the universal principles of democracy and human rights everywhere in the world. This is one of the fundamental tenets of the European common foreign policy. Therefore, we continue to raise human rights issues in the political dialogue with the Thai authorities and in multilateral forums, including at the Human Rights Council. The EU will also continue to support civil society stakeholders to promote dialogue and their effective participation.

Â 
Â Â 
Â
