Â Â JoÃ£o Oliveira (The Left), por escrito. â Condenamos toda e qualquer violaÃ§Ã£o de direitos humanos, seja na TailÃ¢ndia, seja em qualquer paÃ­s que integra a UE, ou noutra qualquer parte do Mundo. Quando a situaÃ§Ã£o da TailÃ¢ndia tem sido praticamente ignorada nos Ãºltimos anos pelo PE, Ã© num momento de aproximaÃ§Ã£o deste paÃ­s Ã  RepÃºblica Popular da China que surge esta resoluÃ§Ã£o, instrumentalizando cinicamente as migraÃ§Ãµes e os uigures (detidos hÃ¡ dez anos em centros de detenÃ§Ã£o tailandeses sem que tal tivesse merecido anteriormente qualquer consideraÃ§Ã£o). A UE nÃ£o tem moral, pelas polÃ­ticas que defende, promove e implementa no que aos migrantes e refugiados diz respeito, para ditar juÃ­zos sobre terceiros quanto a esta questÃ£o. NÃ£o acompanhamos e denunciamos a instrumentalizaÃ§Ã£o dos direitos humanos, para que, a seu pretexto, sejam promovidas polÃ­ticas coercivas de relacionamento ou encobertas e dissimuladas operaÃ§Ãµes de ingerÃªncia externa e polÃ­ticas de confrontaÃ§Ã£o no plano internacional, que visam, alÃ©m da TailÃ¢ndia, a RepÃºblica Popular da China. A relaÃ§Ã£o da UE e dos seus Estados-Membros com outros paÃ­ses deve ser pautada pelo respeito pela sua soberania e independÃªncia, no cumprimento dos princÃ­pios da Carta das NaÃ§Ãµes Unidas e do Direito Internacional, respeitando o direito dos povos a decidir do seu destino, livre de ingerÃªncias externas, promovendo a paz, e uma cooperaÃ§Ã£o mutuamente vantajosa.

Â 

17.2. Severe political, humanitarian and human rights crisis in Sudan, in particular the sexual violence and child rape
Video of the speeches
Â 

Â Â PriekÅ¡sÄdÄtÄjs. â NÄkamais darba kÄrtÄ«bas punkts ir debates par septiÅiem rezolÅ«ciju priekÅ¡likumiem par smago politisko, humanitÄro un cilvÄktiesÄ«bu krÄ«zi SudÄnÄ, jo Ä«paÅ¡i seksuÄlo vardarbÄ«bu un bÄrnu izvaroÅ¡anu (2025/2595(RSP)).

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Lukas Mandl, author. â MrÂ President, Commissioner, colleagues, humanitarian organisations call Sudan and the situation there literally one of the worst humanitarian nightmares of recent history. So, rightly, late at night now in the European Parliament, we use the time to reflect on this worst humanitarian nightmare of recent history.

We all know the Russian attack against Ukraine is close geographically and is also close to our way of life. But there are also other crises on earth. In terms of quantity, Sudan is one of the major crises, and also in terms of cruelty â even sexual violence is used as a weapon of war in this crisis.

Many among the most evil powers on our planet are present in Sudan, among others: Islamist terror, Islamist ideology and violence, as well as Putin's Russian forces who try to exploit the soil and to torture the population there as well.

I thank Hilde Vautmans and the other co-negotiators on this file and I'm happy that we explicitly mention the World Food Programme in this file, which has rightly received the Nobel Peace Prize a few years ago. Three World Food Programme workers were killed some time ago in Sudan. And we can see that the funding of the World Food Programme today is the same as ten years ago, while the number of starving people is four times as much as that time.

And my proposal today is that maybe it can be, even in these times, a cooperation project between EU and US to provide more funding to the World Food Programme, because fighting starvation must be something that's common ground for the EU and US.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Marta Temido, Autora. â Senhor Presidente, Senhor ComissÃ¡rio, o SudÃ£o vive uma catÃ¡strofe humana sem precedentes desde que, hÃ¡ quase dois anos, as ForÃ§as Armadas do SudÃ£o e as ForÃ§as de Apoio RÃ¡pido iniciaram uma guerra civil. Presos neste confronto estÃ£o milhÃµes de sudaneses, em particular mulheres e crianÃ§as.

A violÃªncia sexual, a violaÃ§Ã£o de mulheres, de meninas, de meninos â incluindo a violaÃ§Ã£o coletiva â sÃ£o sistematicamente utilizadas como arma de guerra, com os seus corpos a tornarem-se parte do campo de batalha.

As NaÃ§Ãµes Unidas consideram o estupro o crime de guerra mais antigo, mais silenciado e menos condenado, mas esta prÃ¡tica tambÃ©m Ã© utilizada para semear o terror.Â Por isso, temos a obrigaÃ§Ã£o de exigir a investigaÃ§Ã£o e responsabilizaÃ§Ã£o dos autores destes crimes. Mas temos tambÃ©m a obrigaÃ§Ã£o de manter o apoio firme Ã s suas vÃ­timas, atravÃ©s de mecanismos e fundos disponÃ­veis no contexto da ajuda humanitÃ¡ria, designadamente de saÃºde.

Podemos nÃ£o ter nas nossas mÃ£os pÃ´r fim Ã  guerra, mas temos nas nossas mÃ£os aliviar o sofrimento das suas vÃ­timas.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Catarina Vieira, author. â Mr President, as the brutal war rages in Sudan, the population is confronted with an unprecedented humanitarian crisis: over 150Â 000 people have been killed, 13Â million were forced to flee their homes and 30Â million are dependent on humanitarian aid. Rape has become a method of warfare, leaving permanent scars and destroying lives.

The sad conclusion is that the Western world is failing humanity in Sudan. USAID cuts deeply hit the most vulnerable people directly, putting their lives at risk. And while the EU is preoccupied with the latest nonsense coming from the White House, the United Arab Emirates delivers weapons to a party that is standing accused of genocide.

The EU must take its responsibility: install the monitoring mission, support the work of the ICC and adopt sanctions against those responsible for the violations. If we believe that human rights are universal, we need to act now!

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Rima Hassan, auteur. â Monsieur le PrÃ©sident, enÂ 2019, les femmes soudanaises ont courageusement pris les rues pour faire la rÃ©volution. Aujourd'hui, elles se suicident par peur de subir des viols. Depuis avrilÂ 2023, la guerre qui oppose les forces armÃ©es soudanaises aux Forces de soutien rapide a dÃ©jÃ  fait des dizaines de milliers de morts, troisÂ millions de rÃ©fugiÃ©s et neufÂ millions de dÃ©placÃ©s internes. La guerre a provoquÃ© une famine, qui risque de s'aggraver depuis que Trump a gelÃ© les fonds de l'USAID. Ce mÃªme Trump sanctionne la CPI au moment oÃ¹ elle mÃ¨ne des enquÃªtes sur des crimes contre l'humanitÃ© et des actes de gÃ©nocide perpÃ©trÃ©s au Soudan.

L'Union europÃ©enne porte, elle aussi, une Ã©norme responsabilitÃ©: en externalisant ses frontiÃ¨res, elle finance des autoritÃ©s qui se rendent coupables de graves violations des droits de l'homme. Les Soudanais et Soudanaises fuient la guerre; sur la route de l'exil, ils trouvent la torture. Depuis les enquÃªtes d'Amnesty International, nous savons Ã©galement que des systÃ¨mes d'armements franÃ§ais sont utilisÃ©s par les Forces de soutien rapide, bien qu'un embargo sur les armes soit dÃ©crÃ©tÃ© par les Nations unies. Il est temps que l'Union europÃ©enne soutienne l'ardente sociÃ©tÃ© civile soudanaise matÃ©riellement, financiÃ¨rement et politiquement.

Je terminerai par le slogan de leur rÃ©volution confisquÃ©e.

(L'oratrice conclut son intervention dans une langue non officielle.)

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Barry Andrews, author. â Mr President, Commissioner JÃ¸rgensen, colleagues, we're having this debate about the tragedy in Sudan at a time when we're dismantling the tools that we would otherwise use to address this issue. We're having it at a time when USAID has been decapitated by the US Government, when the UK has reduced its overseas aid from 0.7Â % of GNI a few years ago down to 0.5Â % and now to 0.3Â %. But the reality is it's about 0.15Â %, when you take account of funding that is applied for domestic migration management.

The same is happening in the Netherlands, where they've halved their overseas aid, and they don't want to talk about gender equality anymore in the Netherlands. In Belgium, they reduced it by a quarter; in Sweden, in Switzerland, and now the German Government will probably do the same thing.

So we can say a lot of fine words in this Chamber, in this Hemicycle â as we've done before â but what we've done is surrender soft power on an industrial scale, surrender influence, and power hates a vacuum. So we have made this decision to retreat from an order of global solidarity and replace it with nothing.

And now we're surprised that you find fragile states like Sudan, which is on the brink of starvation, which is so desperately impacted that the US Government in January declared a genocide. I often think, 'pity the soul that has forgotten how to shudder'; you might call it compassion fatigue. But somehow or other, this is the worst humanitarian crisis in the world.

We have no policy that has addressed this. There has been no statement from the Commission about this collapse in overseas aid. So it's all very well to say fine words here, but until we get a clear statement and establish a floor on European policy on these issues, those fine words will just disappear in dust.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Marit Maij, namens de S&D-Fractie. â Voorzitter, de rijke geschiedenis van Sudan en de rijke bodem van Sudan vormen een schril contrast met de situatie van de Sudanezen zelf. Er valt in Sudan ook wat te halen, zoals goud. Ook voor Ã³nze telefoons, voor Ã³nze sieraden, voor Ã³nze apparaten. Landen, bijvoorbeeld in het Midden-Oosten, maar Ã³Ã³k in de Europese Unie, hebben baat bij de grondstoffen uit Sudan en blijven zo het conflict faciliteren en financieren. De Sudanese gemeenschap is daar het slachtoffer van. Miljoenen gevlucht, losgerukt van hun huis, tienduizenden vermoord. Vrouwen en kinderen worden seksueel misbruikt met als doel gemeenschappen te ontwrichten.

Verkrachting als oorlogswapen. Het is de vierde keer in zes maanden dat we hier aandacht vragen voor Sudan en over de situatie in het land debatteren, nu met een resolutie. De Commissie en de lidstaten moeten meer humanitaire hulp bieden nu USAID is weggevallen. Vooral vrouwenorganisaties direct financieren, landen verantwoordelijk houden die het conflict in stand houden, grip krijgen op de herkomst van onze grondstoffen, vooral ook goud dat onze markt binnenkomt en sancties opleggen aan de personen die verantwoordelijk zijn voor deze vreselijke mensenrechtenschendingen.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â ChloÃ© Ridel (S&D). â Monsieur le PrÃ©sident, c'est l'histoire d'une mÃ¨re qui s'interpose entre les miliciens qui ont forcÃ© la porte de chez elle et ses deux filles de 10 et 17Â ans: Â«Si quelqu'un doit Ãªtre violÃ©e ici, que ce soit moi.Â» C'est l'histoire de huitÂ garÃ§ons partis ramasser des fruits, pris en embuscade, menacÃ©s avec une arme et violÃ©s. L'un d'eux avait sixÂ ans. C'est l'histoire de 130Â femmes qui, en octobre dernier, ont prÃ©fÃ©rÃ© se suicider collectivement, plutÃ´t que de vivre brisÃ©es dans un pays anÃ©anti oÃ¹ elles seraient fatalement violÃ©es. C'est la tragÃ©die du Soudan: 60Â 000Â morts, 14Â millions de blessÃ©s, des violences sexuelles massives, des viols en rÃ©union, des mariages forcÃ©s, des enlÃ¨vements et de l'esclavage sexuel. C'est la litanie des violences de genre commises au Soudan, aujourd'hui, qui visent clairement Ã  l'anÃ©antissement physique et moral d'une population entiÃ¨re.

Quand j'entends que dans cet hÃ©micycle, une partie des dÃ©putÃ©s refusent que nous parlions des droits sexuels et reproductifs des Soudanaises, de leur droit Ã  la contraception et Ã  l'avortement, j'en tremble de honte, parce qu'ils ne savent pas que les femmes qui, lÃ -bas, tombent enceintes aprÃ¨s Ãªtre violÃ©es vont jusqu'Ã  se tuer ou Ã  Ãªtre reniÃ©es par leur propre famille.

Il faut voir qu'au chevet des milices du Soudan, il se trouve des pays Ã©trangers qui les fournissent en armes. Nous attendons de la Commission europÃ©enne qu'elle fasse enfin respecter l'embargo sur les armes, dÃ©crÃ©tÃ© depuis 2004 et qui n'est toujours pas acceptÃ©. Il faut tout faire pour mettre fin Ã  la tragÃ©die des Soudanaises et des Soudanais.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Leire PajÃ­n (S&D). â SeÃ±or presidente, seÃ±orÃ­as, la situaciÃ³n humanitaria en SudÃ¡n es el espejo donde se reflejan todos los fracasos de la comunidad internacional: el impacto del cambio climÃ¡tico, la pobreza extrema de mÃ¡s de la mitad de la poblaciÃ³n, la violaciÃ³n y la violencia sexual sistemÃ¡tica contra niÃ±as y niÃ±os como arma de guerra, la desapariciÃ³n de los hospitales y de cualquier tipo de infraestructura.... uno se pregunta quÃ© mÃ¡s tiene que pasar para que nos tomemos en serio la situaciÃ³n de SudÃ¡n. Porque lo peor de todo es el olvido sistemÃ¡tico al que hemos sometido a los ciudadanos de SudÃ¡n.

Y por si esto fuera poco, entrarÃ¡ en vigor este lunes la decisiÃ³n de la AdministraciÃ³n norteamericana de retirar su ayuda oficial al desarrollo. Y eso obliga a la UniÃ³n Europea a redoblar sus esfuerzos, a estar todavÃ­a mÃ¡s presente si cabe. Hemos hablado hoy de seguridad. Y yo les digo: la seguridad no es solo una cuestiÃ³n de defensa, es una cuestiÃ³n tambiÃ©n de resoluciÃ³n de conflictos y de lucha contra la pobreza.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Hannes Heide (S&D). â Herr PrÃ¤sident, Herr Kommissar! Im Sudan ereignet sich die grÃ¶Ãte humanitÃ¤re Krise der Welt. RegelmÃ¤Ãig und wiederholt beschÃ¤ftigen wir uns hier im Plenum mit dieser Katastrophe unfassbaren AusmaÃes, aber nach wie vor ist keine LÃ¶sung der Situation in Aussicht. Diese Woche haben wir den Internationalen Frauentag hier im Parlament begangen. Gleichzeitig werden im Sudan Frauen und Kinder systematisch vergewaltigt, verschleppt und versklavt. Allein in diesem Jahr hat Unicef Ã¼ber 220Â FÃ¤lle sexueller Gewalt gegen Kinder dokumentiert; einige Opfer sind erst wenige Monate alt. Frauen werden in Lagern gefangen gehalten und vergewaltigt.

Wie reagiert die Welt? Die USA ziehen sich zurÃ¼ckÂ â ein fatales Signal. Mehr und effektive humanitÃ¤re Hilfe ist notwendig. Die Opfer benÃ¶tigen vor allem Schutz, Betreuung und medizinische Versorgung. Nur gezielte MaÃnahmen werden diese unhaltbaren ZustÃ¤nde beenden kÃ¶nnen: ein Waffenembargo, internationale Strafverfolgung, aber auch Sanktionen gegen all jene, die diesen Krieg finanzieren oder, wie die Vereinigten Arabischen Emirate, die paramilitÃ¤rische RSF, die fÃ¼r sexuelle Gewalt verantwortlich ist, mit Waffen versorgen.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Nikos Papandreou (S&D). â MrÂ President,Â Commissioner, it's four months and we've spoken about Sudan three times. We haven't changed much.

The previous speaker said something I agree with: setting up an 'EUAID', but that will take time. It won't happen tomorrow. We need, of course, to come in with humanitarian support.

Tomorrow's vote, though, does have some concrete proposals to send peacekeeping forces right away to protect civilians, create safe spaces for women and children, sanction the commanders and impose an arms embargo on Sudan, as we have done on Darfur.

I would like to remind the Chamber that one of our NATO allies is supplying drones to both sides of the conflict, an incredible profit-making machine from the death of the Sudanese.

So, let's impose our vote tomorrow. Let's ask the international community to send in peacekeeping forces, because this will be a ruined generation borne from violence.

Â 
Â Â 
Â Â 

BrÄ«vais mikrofons

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Murielle Laurent (S&D). â Monsieur le PrÃ©sident, Monsieur le Commissaire, mes chers collÃ¨gues, le dÃ©bat qui vient de se tenir ce soir le montre: il faut que les violences sexuelles, et notamment l'utilisation du viol comme arme de guerre, cessent. L'indiffÃ©rence collective autour de ces actes est tout simplement inadmissible.

De plus, depuis la guerre du Soudan, enÂ 2023, plus d'un million de personnes ont fui les combats vers le Tchad, qui, je le rappelle, fait partie des pays les plus pauvres du monde. Fin fÃ©vrier, je me suis notamment rendue dans le camp d'AdrÃ©, Ã  l'est du Tchad, oÃ¹ rÃ©sident un grand nombre de rÃ©fugiÃ©s soudanais dans des conditions de vie prÃ©caires, malgrÃ© le travail important des ONG. Les besoins en aide humanitaire sont immenses et les financements sont soit supprimÃ©s, comme les financements amÃ©ricains, soit insuffisants, soit menacÃ©s. Nous devons Ãªtre Ã  la hauteur et l'Union europÃ©enne se doit de venir en aide Ã  ces millions de personnes, qui en ont plus que besoin.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Sandra GÃ³mez LÃ³pez (S&D). â SeÃ±or presidente, SudÃ¡n vive una de las peores crisis humanitarias del mundo y hoy quiero hablarles de su peor cara. Actualmente la violencia sexual que sufren niÃ±os y niÃ±as estÃ¡ estremeciendo al mundo. Y me gustarÃ­a decirlo asÃ­, pero no es verdad, porque es uno de los conflictos mÃ¡s invisibles que hoy vivimos mientras hay bebÃ©s de menos de un aÃ±o que estÃ¡n siendo violados.

Y miren, hoy no les quiero hablar como polÃ­tica, les quiero hablar como madre, y me gustarÃ­a pedirles que se pongan en la situaciÃ³n de una madre que no puede proteger a su hijo, de un bebÃ© que ni siquiera es capaz aÃºn de hablar y que ha sufrido una de las peores atrocidades. Y, como decÃ­a antes, este conflicto no llena los grandes titulares. Ni siquiera es protagonista de nuestros debates polÃ­ticos. Y por eso yo quiero pedirles que desde aquÃ­ hoy exijamos justicia. Porque si no somos capaces de proteger a un niÃ±o de un violador con uniforme, de un militar, Â¿quÃ© estamos haciendo exactamente aquÃ­ en este Parlamento?

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Lukas Sieper (NI). â MrÂ President,Â people of Europe, let me be clear: I support the authors in their motion. But I'm also wondering, MrÂ Andrews, you talked about soft power and about humanitarian aid. But are we talking about economic collapse? Are we talking about a pandemic, about political instability or a natural disaster?

MsÂ PajÃ­n said we have to take the situation seriously. I am asking what is a serious reaction to war? MrÂ Heide said we need to protect the victims. How do you protect people from armed killers and rapists? Look up again on this board. It says 'child rape' â child rape. And that is something, in my opinion, where we need to send something named after this Union of ours: Eurofighters.

Â 
Â Â 
Â Â 

(BrÄ«vÄ mikrofona uzstÄÅ¡anos beigas.)

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Dan JÃ¸rgensen, Member of the Commission. â MrÂ President, honourable Members, as we are fast approaching the second anniversary of the war in Sudan, neither belligerent party wants to end it. The responsibility for this human-made calamity rests with the Sudanese Armed Forces and the Rapid Support Forces, as well as their respective affiliated militias.

Because of this war, the people of Sudan are facing a catastrophic humanitarian situation. Sudan is the country with the most people in need: over 30 million. To put this in perspective, Sudan accounts for 10Â % of all people requiring humanitarian aid across the world.Â Yet Sudan represents less than 1Â % of the world's population.

The Sudanese people are facing an unprecedented worsening of the nutrition and food crisis, which is leading to a severe famine, as evidenced in Darfur and Kordofan. Unfortunately, many more regions are likely experiencing famine-like conditions. Again, as a comparison, it is only the third time in the 21st century that famine is declared worldwide.

Sudan is also the epicentre of the largest protection crisis in the world. Appalling reports of atrocious international humanitarian law and human rights violations have been witnessed throughout the country, in particular in the most conflict-affected areas.

To further aggravate things, rape and gender-based violence committed by both belligerent parties are also used as tactics on the battlefield. Some 6.7Â million people are at risk of gender-based violence. Displaced refugees, migrant women and girls and even little boys are particularly vulnerable. Sex crimes and violence in conflict is a war crime.

In Sudan, we have to continue to push for peace. Together with our regional and international partners, including the African Union and the United Nations, we call for an immediate ceasefire and a sustainable political solution to the conflict. The EU continues to engage with both warring parties, mainly through the work of the EU Special Representative for the Horn of Africa. There must be accountability for crimes committed too. This is also part and parcel of rebuilding Sudan once the war is over.

Finally, the EU will keep funding initiatives across the globe that provide survivors with healthcare, legal assistance and psychosocial support. We call on others to do so as well. For the victims, wherever you are, the EU's message is clear: you are not alone.

Â 
Â Â 
Â

BalsoÅ¡ana notiks rÄ«tdien.

Â 

17.3. Unlawful detention and sham trials of Armenian hostages, including high-ranking political representatives from Nagorno-Karabakh, by Azerbaijan
Video of the speeches
Â 

Â Â PriekÅ¡sÄdÄtÄjs. â NÄkamais darba kÄrtÄ«bas punkts ir debates par astoÅiem rezolÅ«ciju priekÅ¡likumiem par armÄÅu Ä·Ä«lnieku, tostarp Kalnu Karabahas augsta ranga politisko pÄrstÄvju, nelikumÄ«go aizturÄÅ¡anu un fiktÄ«vo tiesÄÅ¡anu AzerbaidÅ¾ÄnÄ (2025/2596(RSP)).

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Miriam Lexmann, author. â MrÂ President, over 20 political hostages who were imprisoned during the ethnic cleansing of Christian Armenians from Nagorno-Karabakh are still detained in Azerbaijan. They are facing inhuman conditions and sham trials which could result in their life imprisonment. This is the real face of Aliyev's regime â a regime which oppresses its own citizens and disregards international obligations.

While the European Union comes with a strategic partnership, the regime in Baku continues to threaten its neighbours and world efforts toward regional peace. In the current geopolitical environment, there is a strong need for the EU to defend its values, especially in its closest neighbourhood. Instead, we are again appeasing the oppressor and rewarding Aliyev's actions with enhanced trade. Have we not learned our lesson?

It's time to stop dreaming about a strategic partnership, face reality and act in accordance with our values and principles. We have to take urgent action and address claims and these clear violations of basic human rights, and ensure that the hostages are immediately and unconditionally released. Leading judges and prosecutors who play a role in these unjust trials should face sanctions, and we have to make sure that any future cooperation with Azerbaijan is based on conditions of the release of all the political prisoners and demonstrated readiness of Baku to engage in a peace agreement which would respect the rights of Nagorno-Karabakh Armenians.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Vasile DÃ®ncu, autor. â Domnule preÈedinte, dragi colegi, situaÈia prizonierilor politici armeni din Nagorno-Karabah ridicÄ Ã®ntrebÄri fundamentale despre justiÈie, stabilitate Èi viitorul pÄcii Ã®n Caucazul de Sud.

Desigur, regiunea Caucazului, aÈa cum o descrie James Forsyth, este una din cele mai colorate Èi vii din lume, iar diversitatea intereselor locale complicÄ orice situaÈie diplomaticÄ Èi orice acÈiune diplomaticÄ. TotuÈi, cÃ¢nd este vorba despre pace Èi stabilitate Ã®n Caucaz, Parlamentul European nu s-a abÄtut deloc de la principiile Èi valorile sale.

Suveranitatea, integritatea teritorialÄ Èi independenÈa Armeniei au animat rezoluÈii precum cea din 13 martie sau din 24 octombrie 2024. Din pÄcate, autoritÄÈile azere persistÄ Ã®n Ã®ncÄlcarea drepturilor omului, promovÃ¢nd o retoricÄ agresivÄ Ã®mpotriva Armeniei, persecutÃ¢nd oficiali armeni din Nagorno-Karabah.

Pe acest fond, propunem ca Uniunea Èi statele membre sÄ vegheze asupra proceselor Èi sÄ viziteze ostaticii armeni. Am mers chiar mai departe, susÈinÃ¢nd sancÈiuni Ã®mpotriva oficialilor azeri responsabili pentru abuzurile judiciare. Regimurile sancÈionatorii ale Uniunii ne permit sÄ adoptÄm mÄsuri strict Ã®mpotriva oficialilor, ca un prim pas pentru descurajarea unor abuzuri viitoare.

Perspectivele nu sunt unele pozitive, de aceea, avÃ¢nd Ã®n vedere miÈcÄrile militare din zonÄ, cred cÄ trebuie sÄ nu aÈteptÄm ca o mare tragedie sÄ se Ã®ntÃ¢mple pentru a adopta mÄsuri concrete. Este un fel de Ã®ntrebare pentru Comisie Ã®n acest moment.

Dragi colegi, aÈa cum am mai spus, Caucazul de Sud este o regiune deosebit de complexÄ. Trebuie deci sÄ acÈionÄm cu dibÄcie diplomaticÄ, inteligent, dar ferm, pentru protejarea valorilor noastre Èi a drepturilor omului. Prizonierii nu sunt doar un numÄr, sunt fraÈi, pÄrinÈi Èi copiii cuiva, meritÄ libertate. Eliberarea prizonierilor politici ar trebui sÄ fie parte dintr-un efort mai amplu de construire a unui viitor bazat pe Ã®ncredere reciprocÄ Èi cooperare, dar mai ales pe respect faÈÄ de ordinea globalÄ Èi reguli. Trebuie sÄ luptÄm pentru ca sÄ Èinem aceastÄ viziune Ã®n viaÈÄ.

Â Â Nathalie Loiseau, auteur. â Monsieur le PrÃ©sident, nous sommes rÃ©unis aujourd'hui pour exiger la libÃ©ration des 23Â otages armÃ©niens dÃ©tenus par Bakou et qui font face Ã  un procÃ¨s inique. J'ai voulu cette rÃ©solution et je remercie mes coauteurs. Le procÃ¨s en cours n'a rien Ã  envier aux procÃ¨s staliniens. Les accusÃ©s sont privÃ©s de tout et subissent des mauvais traitements. Leur seul crime est d'Ãªtre ArmÃ©niens.

Je voudrais m'adresser Ã  KajaÂ Kallas, en regrettant qu'une fois de plus, elle ne soit pas lÃ  pour nous Ã©couter. Madame la Haute ReprÃ©sentante, je voudrais vous fÃ©liciter pour l'envoi de matÃ©riel Ã  l'ArmÃ©nie, dans le cadre de la FacilitÃ© europÃ©enne pour la paix. Je voudrais aussi saluer l'extension du mandat de la mission de l'Union europÃ©enne en ArmÃ©nie, EUMA, qui fait un excellent travail. Je vous encourage mÃªme vivement Ã  aller le constater sur le terrain par vous-mÃªme.

Madame la Haute ReprÃ©sentante, j'Ã©tais en ArmÃ©nie rÃ©cemment et j'espÃ¨re que vous vous y rendrez bientÃ´t. Vous y verrez un pays qui veut la paix, qui rÃªve d'Europe, mais qui est confrontÃ© Ã  un voisin, l'AzerbaÃ¯djan, qui continue Ã  le menacer et qui est l'un des meilleurs alliÃ©s de la Russie. J'espÃ¨re que vous rÃ©sisterez Ã  ceux qui vous conseillent l'Ã©quidistance entre Erevan et Bakou. J'espÃ¨re aussi que vous vous souviendrez que l'AzerbaÃ¯djan mÃ¨ne vis-Ã -vis de la France une politique d'ingÃ©rence hostile, qui ne peut pas rester sans consÃ©quence. Je vous demande en clair d'Ã©couter et de mettre en Åuvre ce que ce Parlement vous demande depuis longtemps et avec insistance.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Marina Mesure, auteur. â Monsieur le PrÃ©sident, il y a au moins deuxÂ ans, aux confins de l'Europe, se dÃ©roulait sous nos yeux un crime contre l'humanitÃ© dans un silence assourdissant: plus de 100Â 000Â personnes forcÃ©es Ã  l'exil au Haut-Karabagh parce qu'ArmÃ©niennes; une tentative dÃ©libÃ©rÃ©e du rÃ©gime d'Aliev d'effacer une culture, une histoire et une identitÃ© qui remontent Ã  des millÃ©naires.

Devant cette tragÃ©die, l'Europe n'a pas Ã©tÃ© Ã  la hauteur, en privilÃ©giant son accord gazier avec Bakou. Aujourd'hui, elle peut et elle doit agir pour exiger la libÃ©ration des prisonniers armÃ©niens dÃ©tenus par l'AzerbaÃ¯djan. Ils sont encore des centaines Ã  Ãªtre emprisonnÃ©s et torturÃ©s, dont le seul crime est d'avoir dÃ©fendu leur terre, leur peuple et leurs droits. Ne pas agir, c'est envoyer un message clair Ã  Aliev. C'est dire qu'il peut prolonger sa politique expansionniste Ã  l'encontre de l'ArmÃ©nie, qu'il considÃ¨re comme une province, et cela en toute impunitÃ©. Ainsi, le silence n'est pas une option.

Nous envoyons un message clair: l'ArmÃ©nie n'est pas seule. Nous sommes unis Ã  ses cÃ´tÃ©s pour faire respecter son intÃ©gritÃ© territoriale, sa souverainetÃ© et son droit Ã  vivre en paix et en sÃ©curitÃ©, sans craindre de nouvelle agression de son voisin azerbaÃ¯djanais.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Matthieu Valet, auteur. â Monsieur le PrÃ©sident, une fois de plus, nous dÃ©plorons et condamnons les agissements hostiles de l'AzerbaÃ¯djan Ã  l'encontre des ArmÃ©niens du Haut-Karabagh. Malheureusement, nous savons d'avance que ce dÃ©bat ainsi que la rÃ©solution qui suivra tomberont dans une oreille sourde, car l'accord gazier signÃ© par la Commission europÃ©enne ne sera nullement remis en cause, malgrÃ© nos demandes rÃ©pÃ©tÃ©es. Il est incomprÃ©hensible que l'Union europÃ©enne coupe les flux gaziers en provenance de la Russie, mais garde un silence assourdissant face Ã  l'Ã©puration ethnique qui se dÃ©roule au Haut-Karabagh.

De plus, qu'attend l'Union europÃ©enne pour condamner la posture agressive de l'AzerbaÃ¯djan envers la France, un Ãtat membre, lorsqu'elle voit son intÃ©gritÃ© territoriale menacÃ©e par ce pays, qui soutient les mouvements indÃ©pendantistes violents et diffuse de la dÃ©sinformation en Nouvelle-CalÃ©donie? Ã cette occasion, je tiens Ã  remercier nos policiers, nos gendarmes, nos militaires et nos sapeurs pompiers qui, en Nouvelle-CalÃ©donie, par leur bravoure et leur courage, tÃ©moignent d'un engagement indÃ©fectible pour la dÃ©fense de ce territoire europÃ©en. Il est d'une importance primordiale et stratÃ©gique de dÃ©fendre la souverainetÃ© franÃ§aise de ces territoires de l'Indo-Pacifique. En effet, que serait l'Union europÃ©enne sans cette zone maritime d'une importance primordiale et capitale dans les domaines de la pÃªche, de la science, de la dÃ©fense et de l'Ã©nergie de l'Union europÃ©enne dans le monde?

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Catarina Vieira, author. â MrÂ President, dear colleagues, Azerbaijan is facing a deep human rights crisis. The government installed severe restrictions on freedom of expression, assembly and association, and weaponised the legal system against critical voices.

Armenian detainees are one of the groups most severely affected by the government's policies. Ill-treatment is widespread and fair trials have become an illusion.

I am glad that this Parliament is consistently and strongly addressing the situation, but sadly we seem to be the only EU institution doing so. We cannot allow continuing business as usual with Azerbaijan, sacrificing our values because of economic and energy interests. This is not only hypocritical, but it generates dependencies, making us vulnerable for blackmail.

Have we not learned any lesson from our dependencies with Russia in the past? It is time we prioritise human rights above cheap oil and gas.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Tomasz Froelich, Verfasser. â Herr PrÃ¤sident! Die Armenier sind ein stolzes Volk, das einem nur leid tun kann: schlecht regiert von Nikol Paschinjan, angegriffen von Aserbaidschan und im Stich gelassen von der EU, aber auch von Russland. In den letzten Jahren hat Baku Fakten geschaffen: Angriff auf Berg-Karabach, AuflÃ¶sung der Republik Arzach, hunderte Tote, Ã¼ber 100Â 000 Armenier auf der Flucht, viele Gefangene, viele GeiselnÂ â bis heute. Hinzu kommt die systematische ZerstÃ¶rung des kulturellen Erbes des ersten christlichen Staates der Welt. Warum hÃ¶rt man eigentlich so wenig davon?

Vielleicht, weil europÃ¤ische Politiker bei alledem eine beschÃ¤mende Rolle spielen. Gegen Politiker der CDU wurde Anklage erhoben. Der Vorwurf: Bestechung durch die aserbaidschanische Regierung, 4Â Millionen Euro Schmiergeld, damit deutsche Christdemokraten wegschauen, wenn armenische Christen vertrieben werden. KaviarâDiplomatie nennt sich dasÂ â eine Schande! Europaweit sollen sogar Ã¼ber 30Â Millionen Euro geflossen sein. Das muss diese wertegeleitete AuÃenpolitik sein, von der hier stÃ¤ndig die Rede ist.

StabilitÃ¤t im SÃ¼dkaukasus ist auch in unserem Interesse. Diplomatie, FriedensgesprÃ¤che und eine respektvolle Behandlung der armenischen Geiseln, des armenischen Volkes und seines christlichen Erbes sind die Voraussetzung dafÃ¼r.

Porusza temat bezprawnego zatrzymania i pokazowych procesÃ³w ormiaÅskich zakÅadnikÃ³w, w tym wysokich rangÄ przedstawicieli politycznych z GÃ³rskiego Karabachu. Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Michael McNamara, on behalf of the Renew Group. â Mr President, colleagues, the deterioration in respect for human rights in Azerbaijan should be a source of concern to this Parliament. But so, too, should the Commission's silence in the face of that.

The ongoing detention and trial of 16Â persons from Nagorno-Karabakh, including eight former leaders, must be condemned for what it is: a Stalinist show trial. So, too, the denial of access to the media and observers, even though the trials are supposedly being held in public.

This is exacerbated by Azerbaijan's ongoing refusal to cooperate with the European Committee for the Prevention of Torture and Inhuman or Degrading Treatment, and this, too, should be noted by this Parliament and the European Commission. The Red Cross, the only external organisation with access to these prisoners, has now been asked to withdraw from Azerbaijan and this, too, needs to be noted.

It does seem that the Commission is only capable of condemning one country at a time, and that should not be the case when there is such a clear deterioration in standards, as there is in Azerbaijan.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Pernando Barrena Arza, on behalf of The Left Group. â MrÂ President, Azerbaijan holds the painful record of having made a country disappear overnight without the international community paying too much attention. It's almost two years since Azerbaijan completed an ethnic cleansing of the entire Armenian population of Nagorno-Karabakh: it emptied the country of Armenians and replaced them with Azeri individuals. Furthermore, we cannot forget that Azerbaijan is an authoritarian state where any legitimate political dissent is cracked down.

We must demand the release of the 23 Armenian prisoners of war who are being subjected to sham trials, violations of due process and trumpedâup charges. In this regard, it's particularly important for the International Criminal Court to investigate the cases submitted by the Ombudsman of Nagorno-Karabakh and provide a judicial response to the cases of forced expulsion and ethnic cleansing committed by Azerbaijan against the Armenian population of Artsakh.

As this violent approach by Azerbaijan has the whole Armenian community in its sights, I have a direct question to High Commissioner Kallas: are you ready to impose the same sanctions against Aliyev if he invades Armenia, as the European Union has imposed against Putin after the invasion of Ukraine?

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Massimiliano Salini (PPE). â Signor Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, lo Stato dell'Azerbaigian continua indisturbato le proprie violenze nei confronti del popolo armeno. Dopo l'invasione militare del settembre 2023 nel Nagorno Karabakh e la relativa deportazione di 100.000 cittadini, sono iniziati queste detenzioni illegali di 23 cittadini armeni e, oggi, questi processi totalmente opachi.

Le colpe che non non vengono perdonate al popolo armeno sono di due nature: la prima Ã¨ geografica: interrompere la continuitÃ  territoriale tra Azerbaigian e Turchia; e la seconda, Ã¨ culturale: il popolo armeno Ã¨ un popolo cristiano.

Le domande che il popolo armeno rivolge all'Europa sono domande degne di un popolo europeo. Diciamolo chiaramente! Questo significa, che a noi europei, Ã¨ chiesto di valutare se siamo disposti a riservare agli azeri lo stesso trattamento che riserviamo ai russi per come non rispettano la libertÃ  dell'Ucraina.

Sanzioni vere e la libertÃ  per questi 23 cittadini illegalmente detenuti.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â FranÃ§ois-Xavier Bellamy (PPE). â Monsieur le PrÃ©sident, Monsieur le Commissaire, le comble de l'indÃ©cence est atteint. Aliev attaque le Haut-Karabagh. Aliev s'en prend Ã  une population qui ne demandait rien d'autre que de vivre en paix sur sa terre, sur la terre sur laquelle elle vit depuis des millÃ©naires maintenant. Aliev dÃ©porte des populations civiles. Aliev utilise des bombes Ã  sous-munitions et des mercenaires djihadistes. Au terme de toute cette horreur, que fait-il? Il met en accusation ses victimes. Aujourd'hui, 23Â prisonniers armÃ©niens sont soumis Ã  un simulacre de procÃ¨s, qui rappelle les grandes heures du stalinisme.Â Que fait l'Europe? L'Europe se tait. Nous l'avons dÃ©jÃ  tellement dit.

Monsieur le Commissaire, vous Ãªtes responsable de l'Ã©nergie. Comment pouvons-nous, comment pouvez-vous justifier qu'aujourd'hui, l'Europe importe du gaz d'AzerbaÃ¯djan avec un contrat particulier avec ce Â«partenaire fiableÂ», pour reprendre les mots de la Commission europÃ©enne? Comment pouvons-nous justifier que les criminels courent encore en Europe, et que le gaz coule encore vers nos pays? Est-ce que c'est plus indÃ©cent aujourd'hui de faire venir du gaz de Bakou que du gaz de Moscou? Est-ce que ce n'est pas le mÃªme gaz? Monsieur le Commissaire, nous avons besoin d'une rÃ©ponse sur cette question.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Dan JÃ¸rgensen, Member of the Commission. â MrÂ President, honourable Members, like this House, the Commission is following the trials against the 16 Karabakh Armenian defendants that are taking place at the Baku Military Court since 17Â January of this year. We are aware that human rights organisations have expressed concerns over the fairness of the trials and treatments of the accused. Many of you have expressed similar concerns here today.

We take these concerns very seriously and are closely following the developments. The right to a fair trial must be ensured and respected. The EU Special Representative for the South Caucasus is also closely following related issues and relaying our concerns to the authorities in Baku.

Our relationship with Azerbaijan is founded on principles both sides have committed themselves to. First and foremost, respect for human rights and the rule of law. The European Union will therefore continue to call on the government of Azerbaijan to abide by its international obligations, which include the rights to due process and fair trial. We will use every opportunity to urge Azerbaijan to ensure dignified and safe conditions for detainees. The reports of torture and ill treatment should be promptly and impartially investigated.

The trials of the Baku Military Court touch on the much larger question of achieving lasting and sustainable peace between Armenia and Azerbaijan, of building a peaceful, stable and prosperous South Caucasus. We therefore continue to call on both Armenia and Azerbaijan to build on their achievements and to continue making progress on their path towards full normalisation of relations.

It is important that the governments of both countries take their societies along on the path. The EU will continue to support this process with all the tools at our disposal. We stand ready to help the parties in their dialogue aimed at achieving lasting and sustainable peace.

Honourable Members, I am grateful for this House's persistence in drawing attention to the situation in Azerbaijan and the wider region. It is an expression of our shared commitment to human rights and the rule of law as a guiding principle in our engagement with Azerbaijan and other partners around the world.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â President. â The debate is closed.

The vote will take place tomorrow.

Â 

18. Welcome
Video of the speeches
Â 

Â Â President. â I would like to welcome a delegation from the Parliamentary Assembly of Bosnia and Herzegovina, which is participating in the 6th EU-Bosnia and Herzegovina Stabilisation and Association Parliamentary Committee meeting. It is late at night, but welcome!

Â 

19. Secessionist threats in Bosnia and Herzegovina and the recent escalation (debate)
Video of the speeches
Â 

Â Â PriekÅ¡sÄdÄtÄjs. â NÄkamais darba kÄrtÄ«bas punkts ir debates par Komisijas paziÅojumu par separÄtisma draudiem BosnijÄ un HercegovinÄ un neseno saspÄ«lÄjuma pieaugumu (2025/2606(RSP)).

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Christophe Hansen, Member of the Commission. â MrÂ President, honourable Members, one year has passed since the historic decision of the European Council to open EU accession negotiations with Bosnia and Herzegovina. It seems the reform enthusiasm has evaporated, and Bosnia and Herzegovina has again entered into an institutional and political crisis. To get back on track, we call on all political actors to continue taking resolute action to finalise reforms in line with the EU acquis and European standards. In this regard, it is crucial to continue addressing the relevant steps to move forward on the EU path. It is what the vast majority of the citizens of Bosnia and Herzegovina expect.

The Commission continuously supports the country in this endeavour. Despite the turbulence in the ruling majority, the adoption in Parliament on 30Â January of the Law on Personal Data Protection â a precondition for cooperation with Eurojust â and of the Law on Border Control was a good signal into the right direction.

We need further progress and this means, in particular, the adoption of the Law on Courts and the Law on High Judicial and Prosecutorial Council being fully in line with European standards. The appointment of a chief negotiator also remains important. Let us also remind Bosnia and Herzegovina not to lose the opportunity of over EURÂ 1 billion of investments through the Growth Plan for the Western Balkans. Therefore, it is important for the country to submit its reform agenda without further delay. It is even more important in view of the Western Balkans Leaders' Summit on the Growth Plan in Skopje next week on 19 and 20 March.

Contrary to the progress on the EU path, recent actions by the Republika Srpska leadership following the first instance ruling in the criminal trial against Milorad Dodik on 26Â February present a serious setback. On Thursday 27 February, the Republika Srpska National Assembly adopted four laws in urgent procedure, including to ban state level judicial and police bodies from operating in the entity, to set up a separate Judiciary Council and to target NGOs as foreign agents. These legal acts undermine the constitutional and legal order of Bosnia and Herzegovina and the functionality of its institutions. Specifically, the Republika Srpska law on foreign agents adversely affects democratic values and threatens fundamental freedoms, marking a step backwards.

These actions directly contradict the repeated commitments of the Republika Srpska entity leadership to advance on the EU accession path of Bosnia and Herzegovina. The EU therefore urges all political actors to renew the focus on progressing on the EU path. On Friday 7Â March, the Constitutional Court of Bosnia and Herzegovina issued interim measures suspending the application of all legal acts adopted by the Republika Srpska National Assembly on 27Â February.

As we have recalled on several occasions, the EU expects all political actors to respect the decision of the Court of Bosnia and Herzegovina, to acknowledge its independence and impartiality, and to refrain from actions that may deepen divisions and create further tensions.

Further, last week, the operational commander of EUFOR-Althea decided to temporarily increase the size of its force by activating the reserves as a proactive measure aimed at assisting Bosnia and Herzegovina in the interest of all citizens. An increased and visible EUFOR presence is directly related to its task of supporting the authorities of Bosnia and Herzegovina in ensuring a safe and secure environment for all citizens.

Dear colleagues, to conclude, the EU answer has been firm and unequivocal. We now expect the Republika Srpska authorities to respect the decision of the Constitutional Court of Bosnia and Herzegovina to suspend the laws threatening the legal order. We urge all political actors to enter into dialogue rapidly, to de-escalate the situation and to focus efforts on delivering progress for citizens, including on the EU path.

Progress on the EU path is the best guarantee for stability in Bosnia and Herzegovina, and has multiple benefits for the citizens of the country. The Commission remains committed to Bosnia and Herzegovina's accession perspective as a single, united and sovereign country. Thank you for your attention and I am looking forward to a fruitful discussion.

Â 
Â Â 
Â

Å½Ã¡dÃ¡ Rusko, aby na pÅ¯dÄ SpojenÃ½ch nÃ¡rodÅ¯ hlasovalo proti zÃ¡vÄrÅ¯m DaytonskÃ© dohody a tomu, co vlastnÄ v BosnÄ a HercegovinÄ udrÅ¾uje mÃ­r.

My jsme teÄ v situaci, kdy do Bosny a Hercegoviny musÃ­me posÃ­lat vojÃ¡ky, aby se neopakovalo to, na co jsem se jako malÃ½ kluk dÃ­val v televiznÃ­ch zprÃ¡vÃ¡ch v polovinÄ devadesÃ¡tÃ½ch let. JÃ¡ chci, abychom tam mohli posÃ­lat prostÅedky na vÃ½stavbu Å¡kol, Å¡kolek, nemocnic, infrastruktury, a mimo jinÃ© proto se s kolegynÄmi a kolegy z EvropskÃ©ho parlamentu chystÃ¡me na zaÄÃ¡tku dubna na cestu do Bosny, abychom se na vlastnÃ­ oÄi pÅesvÄdÄili, jakÃ½ je stav vÄcÃ­, a podle toho tady dÃ¡l mohli pracovat na tom, aby se Bosna a Hercegovina ÃºspÄÅ¡nÄ stala Älenem EvropskÃ© unie.

Â 
Â Â

Â Â MatjaÅ¾ Nemec, v imenu skupine S&D. â Gospod predsednik. Dragi prijatelji iz Bosne in Hercegovine. Bosna in Hercegovina si zasluÅ¾i vso pozornost mednarodne skupnosti in Å¡e posebej Evropske unije, ki je do danes v drÅ¾avo in njeno evropsko integracijo vloÅ¾ila veliko energije in politiÄnega kapitala, kot tudi moja drÅ¾ava, Republika Slovenija.

A dejstvo je, da Milorad Dodik uresniÄuje separatistiÄne groÅ¾nje. Njegove poteze pa resno ogroÅ¾ajo ozemeljsko celovitost in suverenost Bosne in Hercegovine, prav tako pa tudi njeno evropsko prihodnost.

Poglabljanju destabilizacije in varnostnih tveganj se lahko uÄinkovito zoperstavimo. BoleÄine Bosne in Hercegovine Evropi niso tuje. Zgodovina pa ponuja mnogokatere reÅ¡itve.

Evropska unija mora tako konstruktiven partner postati in biti, ki si aktivno prizadeva za vzpostavitev dialoga z vsemi tremi narodi v drÅ¾avi ter za omehÄanje destruktivnega in moÄnega kremeljskega vpliva na Dodikovo Republiko Srbsko.

ZavedajoÄ se, kaj je na kocki, in moralna dolÅ¾nost tako Unije in politiÄnih akterjev v Bosni in Hercegovini je vnoviÄ doseÄi notranjo spravo, s tem pa odpreti pot konsenzu o evropski poti in reformni agendi. To je navsezadnje tudi predpogoj za vzpostavitev predvidenih finanÄnih sredstev za Bosno in Hercegovino iz naÄrta za rast za Zahodni Balkan.

Zato, spoÅ¡tovani komisar, kolegice in kolegi, Unija mora ostati v srediÅ¡Äu politiÄnega dogajanja in stopiti korak dlje. ReÅ¡itev za Bosno je polnopravno Älanstvo v EU, ne veÄ samo v Daytonu.

Dragim prijateljem v Bosni in Hercegovini pa Å¾elim poloÅ¾iti na srce, da je zdaj skrajni Äas za politiÄno zrelost in konstruktivizem. Samo skupaj lahko premikamo meje Evropske unije in ohranimo mir in stabilnost. SreÄno.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â AndrÃ© RougÃ©, au nom du groupe PfE. â Monsieur le PrÃ©sident, Mesdames et Messieurs les reprÃ©sentants du Parlement de Bosnie-HerzÃ©govine, chers collÃ¨gues, la demande d'interpellation du prÃ©sident de l'entitÃ© serbe, MiloradÂ Dodik, par le parquet fÃ©dÃ©ral de Bosnie-HerzÃ©govine n'est pas seulement une infamie; c'est aussi une faute grave. Dans le contexte dÃ©jÃ  troublÃ© de notre continent, le devoir et la responsabilitÃ© de l'Union europÃ©enne sont de stabiliser les Balkans occidentaux, et non de jeter de l'huile sur le feu, en rajoutant un nouveau foyer de tensions en Bosnie-HerzÃ©govine.

Par consÃ©quent, cessons de nous acharner contre BanjaÂ Luka, en violation de l'esprit de Dayton. Cessons de soutenir des mesures provocatrices, comme les poursuites judiciaires, en rÃ©alitÃ© de nature politique, lancÃ©es par une Cour constitutionnelle et un parquet illÃ©gitimes aux yeux de la Republika Srpska. Cessons surtout d'appliquer un Â«deux poids, deux mesuresÂ», qui n'honore pas l'Union europÃ©enne et risque de rouvrir les plaies du passÃ©. Appliquons donc les accords de Dayton, rien que les accords de Dayton et tous les accords de Dayton. DÃ©ployons toutes les virtualitÃ©s de coexistence et de dÃ©veloppement qu'il recÃ¨le, plutÃ´t que de nous livrer Ã  la pyromanie.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Abir Al-Sahlani, on behalf of the Renew Group. â Mr President, there will be a time when it is too late to act. Bosnia and Herzegovina is wrapped up in a conflict that is driven by Milorad Dodik and his boy band: Putin, OrbÃ¡n and Vucic. Together they struck a serious blow to the Dayton peace agreement.

Mr President, Commissioner, esteemed colleagues, let us not fail the people of Bosnia again. We failed them as we watched the first genocide after the Holocaust unroll in front of our eyes in July 1995, in Srebrenica. We failed them when we watched concentration camps built where Bosnians were systematically murdered, tortured, raped, starved. We failed them when we watched Sarajevo be under siege for three long years.

The time for press releases is over. It's time to act! Impose swift sanctions on those who are breaching the Dayton peace agreement. Strengthen our EUFOR mission so we can keep the peace. And continue the support to the Bosnian legal system and institutions, with the aim for them to become members of the EU. Do not let the people of Bosnia down again. Never forget Srebrenica!

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Tineke Strik, on behalf of the Verts/ALE Group. â MrÂ President, the latest illegal moves towards succession by MrÂ Dodik are a direct threat to the peace and integrity of Bosnia and Herzegovina and its future into the EU. MrÂ Dodik is not acting in the interest of the citizens of Republika Srpska or their well-being. Acting as Putin's puppet on the Balkans, Dodik stands ready to fulfil any request from Moscow, even if this means war affecting the citizens he claims to represent. He's a convicted criminal whose only goal is to sustain power and enrich himself through corrupted politics.

The EU must impose sanctions on Dodik and his partners in crime, freeze foreign assets and issue entry bans and, if needed, without Hungary, to protect the security of the Bosnian and Herzegovinian and the European people. We must, and we can, offer a real perspective to all citizens of Bosnia and Herzegovina if we respect the rule of law, make these corrupted politicians harmless and start listening to the will of the citizens.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Michael Gahler (PPE). â MrÂ President, colleagues, Commissioner, in Milorad Dodik's wet dreams, he sees Bosnia and Herzegovina disintegrate and he leads the Republika Srpska into a Greater Serbia. That is why he acts the way he acts.

The law on the protection of the constitutional order would, if adopted and implemented, actually result in the opposite of what it claims in its title. Today they initiated the adoption of a new constitution. A special session will be held tomorrow to adopt the draft constitution and the law.

The RS is clearly not abiding by the BiH Constitutional Court interim measures to annul earlier decisions of the RS Assembly of 27Â February. But fortunately, Dodik is quite alone. Not even the relevant opposition parties in the RS support him. His best supporter, whom he visited at least three times last year, is Vladimir Putin, who is always willing to promote any activities that can cause us trouble.

It is good to see that we are upgrading our EUFOR military presence in the country as a preventive measure. A contingent from the Czech Republic, Romania and Italians are arriving. From the European Commission and other executive bodies, I expect that they will be very explicit. You did it with regard to our demands towards all stakeholders, including Dodik. You have to accept that the framework for policymaking is the Dayton Agreement, the constitution of Bosnia and Herzegovina, and that includes the respect of the verdicts of the Constitutional Court.

What was started today is clearly anti-constitutional. If the constitutional authorities of BiH can eventually not prevent this process being finalised, it would be up to Christian Schmidt to act. I hope it is not necessary, but we have also possibilities from Brussels to have appropriate pressure to apply.

Can we afford to send funds to an anti-constitutional structure? Can you prevent that? I think only together can we make Bosnia and Herzegovina advance towards the EU. And that is what the people ...

(The President cut off the speaker)

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Thijs Reuten (S&D). â MrÂ President, Commission, colleagues, Milorad Dodik's toxic mix of secessionism, autocratic tendencies, cosying up to Putin, ethnic hatred and systemic corruption has finally hit an obstacle.

For months now, he tried to negotiate a way out of an inevitable conviction. Years of neglect, hollowing out of institutions and appeasement out of fear of instability have created a problem that the country â held back by a constitutional straitjacket of power sharing â cannot solve on its own.

Tomorrow, the Commission representatives are on a fool's errand in Bosnia to discuss the growth plan. The Commission should stop appeasement, wishful thinking and pretending that things are fine. They are not.

Instead, hold firm and support Bosnia and Herzegovina's institutions in their duties across the country, be crystal clear about EUFOR's role safeguarding Bosnia and Herzegovina's security and territorial integrity, sanctions against Dodik and those aiding him, and finally support actors who are really willing to work on European integration.

Â 
Â Â 
Â

Mislim, te vijesti sporadiÄno sluÅ¡amo iz godine u godinu, ali danas gledamo i postupke Dodika koji s rijeÄi prelazi na djela. Â Â Å½eljana Zovko (PPE). â MrÂ President, first of all, I would like to say 'hi' to the delegation of parliamentarians from Bosnia and Herzegovina who are watching us. It's quite a shame that we are having this debate so late at night and that there's no interest in Bosnia and Herzegovina, because in Bosnia and Herzegovina, there is no war. There is no incident. But it's a conflict waiting to happen for the past 30 years.

Â Â Reinhold Lopatka (PPE). â Herr PrÃ¤sident, liebe Kollegen! 30Â Jahre nach dem Ende des Bosnienkriegs mit 100Â 000 Opfern steht Bosnien und Herzegowina erneut an einem entscheidenden Punkt seiner Geschichte. Andererseits untergrÃ¤bt Milorad Dodik systematisch die staatlichen Institutionen, verweigert die Umsetzung von Entscheidungen des Verfassungsgerichts und droht immer wieder mit der Abspaltung der Republika Srpska.

Â Â Tomislav Sokol (PPE). â PoÅ¡tovani predsjedavajuÄi, dok jedni u BiH gledaju prema Moskvi i Beogradu, a drugi prema Ankari, Hrvati dosljedno zagovaraju europske vrijednosti poput demokracije i jednakopravnosti.

Danas se Äesto govori o opasnosti od separatizma, a manje o drugom problemu u BiH, a to je unitarizam. PokuÅ¡aji da se BiH centralizira i podredi volji Sarajeva razaraju njezinu unutarnju stabilnost i negiraju temeljno naÄelo na kojem poÄiva, a to je konstitutivnost triju naroda.

Ono Å¡to u velikoj mjeri doprinijelo danaÅ¡njoj situaciji jesu pogreÅ¡ne politike Zapada iz proÅ¡losti koje su godinama poticale unitaristiÄke ambicije, mada upravo to najviÅ¡e destabilizira BiH. Umjesto da podrÅ¾e pravedan i odrÅ¾iv model konsocijacije, Äesto su podrÅ¾avali rjeÅ¡enja koja vode k dominaciji jednog naroda, a time i nestabilnosti. Sjetimo se samo Berija i PetriÄa. Zato je danas kljuÄno inzistirati na poÅ¡tivanju Daytonskog sporazuma i prava svih konstitutivnih naroda na legitimne predstavnike, Å¡to ukljuÄuje pravo Hrvata da napokon sami izaberu svojeg Älana predsjedniÅ¡tva drÅ¾ave.

Stabilna BiH moÅ¾e postojati samo ako se poÅ¡tuje konstitutivnost naroda i u tome Hrvati ostaju kljuÄni nositelji europske perspektive i istinski partneri EU-a.

Â 
Â Â 
Â Â 

(BrÄ«vÄ mikrofona uzstÄÅ¡anos beigas.)

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Christophe Hansen, Member of the Commission. â MrÂ President, honourable Members, many thanks for this debate. The Commission remains unequivocally committed to Bosnia and Herzegovina's EU accession perspective as a single, united and sovereign country. The sovereignty, territorial integrity and constitutional order of Bosnia and Herzegovina must be respected.

Bosnia and Herzegovina has the historic opportunity to open EU accession negotiations. Its citizens want a European future, and it is the responsibility of political actors to make it a reality. We urge all political actors to renew the focus on progressing on the EU path with resolute action to take the relevant steps.

The EU path brings multiple benefits for the country's citizens. Bosnia and Herzegovina is key for the Western Balkans, whose future is in the European Union. EU accession is the agenda with real gains and real benefits for everyone. It is the agenda that brings prosperity, stability and peace. And it is the agenda which we need to work together on.

The Commission will continue supporting Bosnia and Herzegovina in this endeavour with all its instruments. We continue to follow developments very closely, including the possibility of Republika Srpska adopting a new constitution for the entity.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â President. â Thank you very much, Commissioner, and thank you very much to the delegation for being with us so late tonight.

The debate is closed.

Â 

20. Explanations of vote
Video of the speeches
Â 

Â Â President. â The next item is the explanations of vote.

Â 

20.1. The need for EU support towards a just transition and reconstruction in Syria (RC-B10-0157/2025)
Video of the speeches
Â 

Â Â PriekÅ¡sÄdÄtÄjs. â Tagad pievÄrsÄ«simies balsojumu skaidrojumiem par vajadzÄ«bu pÄc ES atbalsta nolÅ«kÄ veicinÄt taisnÄ«gu pÄreju un rekonstrukciju SÄ«rijÄ.Â 

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â Michael McNamara (Renew). â Mr President, I voted in favour of this resolution supporting a just transition and reconstruction in Syria today. The people of Syria have suffered for too long at the hands of a brutal dictator to have it replaced by an equally brutal regime.

I acknowledge the suspension and revision of the sanctions regime, which â we must acknowledge â had little or no impact on Assad's reign and worsened the lives of the ordinary people of Syria, 90Â % of whom now live in poverty.

I look on with great dismay at the persecution and revenge killings of 1Â 225 civilians and members of the Alawite majority faith in recent days, and even greater dismay at the Commission's silence in the face of that killing. The silence of the European Union's chief diplomat on the slaughter of civilians â even if they were once protected by, or may even have supported, leaders that she doesn't like â is a failure on her part; one that lowers her and lowers the EU further on the world stage.

Â 
Â Â 
Â 

Â Â President. â That concludes the explanations of vote.

Â
